verses.verse_tl: sa ya evametā revatyaḥ paśuṣu protā veda paśumānbhavati sarvamāyureti jyogjīvati mahānprajayā paśubhirbhavati mahānkīrtyā paśūnna nindettadvratam ||

verses.meaning_en: The syllable Him is hair, the Prastava skin, the Udgitha flesh, the Pratihara bone, the Nidhana marrow. This is the Yajnayajniya Saman as interwoven in the members of the body.
verses.verse_skt: लोम हिंकारस्त्वक्प्रस्तावो मासमुद्गीथोऽस्थि प्रतिहारो मज्जा निधनमेतद्यज्ञायज्ञीयमङ्गेषु प्रोतम्‌॥
verses.verse_tl: loma hiṁkārastvakprastāvo māsamudgītho'sthi pratihāro majjā nidhanametadyajñāyajñīyamaṅgeṣu protam||

verses.meaning_en: He who thus knows the Yajnayajniya Saman as interwoven in the members of the body becomes possessed of limbs; he is not crippled in any limb, he reaches the full length of life, lives brightly, becomes great in children and cattle, great in fame. For him the injunction is: "For one year do not eat meat" or ''Do not eat meat at all."
verses.verse_skt: स य एवमेतद्यज्ञायज्ञीयमङ्गेषु प्रोतं वेदाऽङ्गी भवति नाङ्गेन विहूर्छति सर्वमायुरेति ज्योग्जीवति महान्प्रजया पशुभिर्भवति महान्कीर्त्या संवत्सरं मज्ज्ञो नाश्नीयात्तद्व्रतं मज्ज्ञो नाश्नीयादिति वा॥
verses.verse_tl: sa ya evametadyajñāyajñīyamaṅgeṣu protaṁ vedā'ṅgī bhavati nāṅgena vihūrchati sarvamāyureti jyogjīvati mahānprajayā paśubhirbhavati mahānkīrtyā saṁvatsaraṁ majjño nāśnīyāttadvrataṁ majjño nāśnīyāditi vā ||

verses.meaning_en: The syllable Him is fire, the Prastava air, the Udgitha the sun, the Pratihara the stars, the Nidhana the moon. This is the Rajana Saman as interwoven in the gods.
verses.verse_skt: अग्निर्हिंकारो वायुः प्रस्ताव आदित्य उद्गीथो नक्षत्राणि प्रतिहारश्चन्द्रमा निधनमेतद्राजनं देवतासु प्रोतम्‌॥
verses.verse_tl: agnirhiṁkāro vāyuḥ prastāva āditya udgītho nakṣatrāṇi pratihāraścandramā nidhanametadrājanaṁ devatāsu protam ||

verses.meaning_en: He who thus knows the Rajana Saman as interwoven in the gods obtains the same world as the gods, acquires the same prosperity as theirs and realizes union with them; he reaches the full length of lives brightly, becomes great in children and cattle, great in fame. him the injunction is: "Do not decry the brahmins."
verses.verse_skt: स य एवमेतद्राजनं देवतासु प्रोतं वेदैतासामेव देवताना सलोकता सर्ष्टिता सायुज्यं गच्छति सर्वमायुरेति ज्योग्जीवति महान्प्रजया पशुभिर्भवति महान्कीर्त्या ब्राह्मणान्न निन्देत्तद्व्रतम्‌॥
verses.verse_tl: sa ya evametadrājanaṁ devatāsu protaṁ vedaitāsāmeva devatānā salokatā sarṣṭitā sāyujyaṁ gacchati sarvamāyureti jyogjīvati mahānprajayā paśubhirbhavati mahānkīrtyā brāhmaṇānna nindettadvratam||

verses.meaning_en: The syllable Him is the three Vedas; the Prastava is these three worlds; the Udgitha is fire (Agni), air (Vayu) and the sun (Aditya); the Pratihara is the stars, the birds and the rays; the Nidhana is the serpents, the gandharvas and the Manes. This is the Saman as interwoven in everything.
verses.verse_skt: त्रयी विद्या हिंकारस्त्रय इमे लोकाः स प्रस्तावोऽग्निर्वायुरादित्यः स उद्गीथो नक्षत्राणि वयासि मरीचयः स प्रतिहारः सर्पा गन्धर्वाः पितरस्तन्निधनमेतत्साम सर्वस्मिन्प्रोतम्‌॥
verses.verse_tl: trayī vidyā hiṁkārastraya ime lokāḥ sa prastāvo'gnirvāyurādityaḥ sa udgītho nakṣatrāṇi vayāsi marīcayaḥ sa pratihāraḥ sarpā gandharvāḥ pitarastannidhanametatsāma sarvasminprotam ||

verses.meaning_en: He who thus knows this Saman as interwoven in everything becomes everything.
verses.verse_skt: स य एवमेतत्साम सर्वस्मिन्प्रोतं वेद सर्व ह भवति॥
verses.verse_tl: sa ya evametatsāma sarvasminprotaṁ veda sarva ha bhavati||

verses.meaning_en: On this there is the following verse: "There are the fivefold three. Greater than these or besides these there is nothing."
verses.verse_skt: तदेष श्लोकः॥ यानि पञ्चधा त्रीणी त्रीणि तेभ्यो न ज्यायः परमन्यदस्ति॥
verses.verse_tl: tadeṣa ślokaḥ || yāni pañcadhā trīṇī trīṇi tebhyo na jyāyaḥ paramanyadasti ||

verses.meaning_en: He who knows this, knows everything. All regions bring him gifts.
verses.verse_skt: यस्तद्वेद स वेद सर्व सर्वा दिशो बलिमस्मै हरन्ति सर्वमस्मीत्युपासित तद्व्रतं तद्व्रतम्‌॥
verses.verse_tl: yastadveda sa veda sarva sarvā diśo balimasmai haranti sarvamasmītyupāsita tadvrataṁ tadvratam||

verses.meaning_en: An Udgatri priest thinks thus: "I choose the deepósounding note of the Saman, which is good for the cattle and which belongs to fire (Agni). The undefined note belongs to Prajapati, the defined note to Soma (the moon), the soft and smooth note to Vayu (the air), the smooth and strong note to Indra, the heronólike note to Brihaspati and dull note to Varuna." Let a man cultivate all these, avoiding, however, the note of Varuna.
verses.verse_skt: विनर्दि साम्नो वृणे पशव्यमित्यग्नेरुद्गीथोऽनिरुक्तः प्रजापतेर्निरुक्तः सोमस्य मृदु श्लक्ष्णं वायोः श्लक्ष्णं बलवदिन्द्रस्य क्रौञ्चं बृहस्पतेरपध्वान्तं वरुणस्य तान्सर्वानेवोपसेवेत वारुणं त्वेव वर्जयेत्‌॥
verses.verse_tl: vinardi sāmno vṛṇe paśavyamityagnerudgītho'niruktaḥ prajāpaterniruktaḥ somasya mṛdu ślakṣṇaṁ vāyoḥ ślakṣṇaṁ balavadindrasya krauñcaṁ bṛhaspaterapadhvāntaṁ varuṇasya tānsarvānevopaseveta vāruṇaṁ tveva varjayet ||

verses.meaning_en: A man should sing, wishing that by his song he may secure immortality for the gods: "May I obtain by my song oblations (svadha) for the Manes, hope for men, grass and water for cattle, heaven for the sacrificer and food for myself." Thus reflecting on all these in his mind, he (the udgatri priest) should chant the praises without making mistakes in pronunciation etc.
verses.verse_skt: अमृतत्वं देवेभ्य आगायानीत्यागायेत्स्वधां पितृभ्य आशां मनुष्येभ्यस्तृणोदकं पशुभ्यः स्वर्गं लोकं यजमानायान्नमात्मन आगायानीत्येतानि मनसा ध्यायन्नप्रमत्तः स्तुवीत॥
verses.verse_tl: amṛtatvaṁ devebhya āgāyānītyāgāyetsvadhāṁ pitṛbhya āśāṁ manuṣyebhyastṛṇodakaṁ paśubhyaḥ svargaṁ lokaṁ yajamānāyānnamātmana āgāyānītyetāni manasā dhyāyannapramattaḥ stuvīta ||

verses.meaning_en: All vowels belong to the different parts of Indra's body, all sibilants to Prajapati, all consonants to Mrityu (death). If someone should reprove him (i.e. the udgatri priest who knows this) regarding the pronunciation of vowels, let him say: "I went to Indra for my refuge when pronouncing my vowels. He will answer you."
verses.verse_skt: सर्वे स्वरा इन्द्रस्यात्मानः सर्व ऊष्माणः प्रजापतेरात्मानः सर्वे स्पर्शा मृत्योरात्मानस्तं यदि स्वरेषूपालभेतेन्द्र शरणं प्रपन्नोऽभूवं स त्वा प्रति वक्ष्यतीत्येनं ब्रूयात्‌॥
verses.verse_tl: sarve svarā indrasyātmānaḥ sarva ūṣmāṇaḥ prajāpaterātmānaḥ sarve sparśā mṛtyorātmānastaṁ yadi svareṣūpālabhetendra śaraṇaṁ prapanno'bhūvaṁ sa tvā prati vakṣyatītyenaṁ brūyāt||

verses.meaning_en: And if someone should reprove him for his sibilants, let him say: "I went to Prajapati for my refuge. He will smash you." And if someone should reprove him for his consonants, let him say" I went to Mrityu for my refuge. He will burn you to ashes."
verses.verse_skt: अथ यद्येनमूष्मसूपालभेत प्रजापति शरणं प्रपन्नोऽभूवं स त्वा प्रति पेक्ष्यतीत्येनंब्रूयादथ यद्येन स्पर्शेषूपालभेत मृत्यु शरणं प्रपन्नोऽभूवं स त्वा प्रति धक्ष्यतीत्येनं ब्रूयात्‌॥
verses.verse_tl: atha yadyenamūṣmasūpālabheta prajāpati śaraṇaṁ prapanno'bhūvaṁ sa tvā prati pekṣyatītyenaṁ brūyādatha yadyena sparśeṣūpālabheta mṛtyu śaraṇaṁ prapanno'bhūvaṁ sa tvā prati dhakṣyatītyenaṁ brūyāt||

verses.meaning_en: All vowels should be pronounced with resonance and strength and with the thought on the part of the singer: "May I impart strength to Indra (the prana)." All the sibilants should be pronounced fullówithout being swallowed or thrown out and with the thought: "May I give myself to Prajapati." All consonants should be pronounced slowly and without mixing them with the others and with the thought: "May I withdraw myself from death."
verses.verse_skt: सर्वे स्वरा घोषवन्तो बलवन्तो वक्तव्या इन्द्रे बलं ददानीति सर्व ऊष्माणोऽग्रस्ता अनिरस्ता विवृता वक्तव्याः प्रजापतेरात्मानं परिददानीति सर्वे स्पर्शा लेशेनानभिनिहिता वक्तव्या मृत्योरात्मानं परिहराणीति॥
verses.verse_tl: sarve svarā ghoṣavanto balavanto vaktavyā indre balaṁ dadānīti sarva ūṣmāṇo'grastā anirastā vivṛtā vaktavyāḥ prajāpaterātmānaṁ paridadānīti sarve sparśā leśenānabhinihitā vaktavyā mṛtyorātmānaṁ pariharāṇīti ||

verses.meaning_en: There are three divisions of dharma: Sacrifice, study and charity form the first. Austerity is the second. Dwelling in the house of the teacher as a brahmacharin, always mortifying the body in the house of the teacher, is the third. All those who practise these dharmas attain the worlds of the virtuous. But one who is established in Brahman obtains Immortality.
verses.verse_skt: त्रयो धर्मस्कन्धा यज्ञोऽध्ययनं दानमिति प्रथमस्तप एव द्वितीयो ब्रह्मचार्याचार्यकुलवासी तृतीयोऽत्यन्तमात्मानमाचार्यकुलेऽवसादयन्सर्व एते पुण्यलोका भवन्ति ब्रह्मसस्थोऽमृतत्वमेति॥
verses.verse_tl: trayo dharmaskandhā yajño'dhyayanaṁ dānamiti prathamastapa eva dvitīyo brahmacāryācāryakulavāsī tṛtīyo'tyantamātmānamācāryakule'vasādayansarva ete puṇyalokā bhavanti brahmasastho'mṛtatvameti||

verses.meaning_en: Prajapati brooded on the worlds. From them, thus brooded upon, there was revealed in his heart the threefold knowledge. He brooded on it and from it, thus brooded upon, there issued forth these syllables: Bhuh, Bhuvah and Svah.
verses.verse_skt: प्रजापतिर्लोकानभ्यतपत्तेभ्योऽभितप्तेभ्यस्त्रयी विद्या संप्रास्रवत्तामभ्यतपत्तस्या अभितप्ताया एतान्यक्षराणि संप्रास्र्वन्त भूर्भुवः स्वरिति॥
verses.verse_tl: prajāpatirlokānabhyatapattebhyo'bhitaptebhyastrayī vidyā saṁprāsravattāmabhyatapattasyā abhitaptāyā etānyakṣarāṇi saṁprāsrvanta bhūrbhuvaḥ svariti ||

verses.meaning_en: He brooded on them (the three syllables) and from them, thus brooded upon, there issued forth Om. As all leaves are held together by a midrib, so is all speech held together by Om (brahman). Om is all thus, yea, On is all this.
verses.verse_skt: तान्यभ्यतपत्तेभ्योऽभितप्तेभ्य ॐकारः संप्रास्रवत्तद्यथा शङ्कुना सर्वाणि पर्णानि संतृण्णान्येवमोंकारेण सर्वा वाक्संतृण्णोंकार एवेद सर्वमोंकार एवेद सर्वम्॥
verses.verse_tl: tānyabhyatapattebhyo'bhitaptebhya omkāraḥ saṁprāsravattadyathā śaṅkunā sarvāṇi parṇāni saṁtṛṇṇānyevamoṁkāreṇa sarvā vāksaṁtṛṇṇoṁkāra eveda sarvamoṁkāra eveda sarvam||

verses.meaning_en: The expounders of Brahman (i.e. the Vedas) ask: "Since the morning oblation belongs to the Vasus, the midday oblation to the Rudras and the third (i.e. evening) oblation to the Adityas and the Visveódevas, "Where, then, is the world of the sacrificer?" He who does not know this, how can he perform the sacrifice? Only he who knows should perform it.
verses.verse_skt: ब्रह्मवादिनो वदन्ति यद्वसूनां प्रातः सवन रुद्राणां माध्यंदिन सवनमादित्यानां च विश्वेषां च देवानां तृतीयसवनम्‌॥
verses.verse_tl: brahmavādino vadanti yadvasūnāṁ prātaḥ savana rudrāṇāṁ mādhyaṁdina savanamādityānāṁ ca viśveṣāṁ ca devānāṁ tṛtīyasavanam ||

verses.meaning_en: The expounders of Brahman (i.e. the Vedas) ask: "Since the morning oblation belongs to the Vasus, the midday oblation to the Rudras and the third (i.e. evening) oblation to the Adityas and the Visveódevas, "Where, then, is the world of the sacrificer?" He who does not know this, how can he perform the sacrifice? Only he who knows should perform it.
verses.verse_skt: क्व तर्हि यजमानस्य लोक इति स यस्तं न विद्यात्कथं कुर्यादथ विद्वान्कुर्यात्‌॥
verses.verse_tl: kva tarhi yajamānasya loka iti sa yastaṁ na vidyātkathaṁ kuryādatha vidvānkuryāt ||

verses.meaning_en: Before beginning the morning chant, the sacrificer, sitting behind the Garhapatya Fire and facing the north, sings the Saman addressed to the Vasus: "O Fire! Open the door of the earthóworld. Let us see thee, that we may rule this earth.
verses.verse_skt: पुरा प्रातरनुवाकस्योपाकरणाज्जघनेन गार्हपत्यस्योदाङ्मुख उपविश्य स वासव सामाभिगायति॥
verses.verse_tl: purā prātaranuvākasyopākaraṇājjaghanena gārhapatyasyodāṅmukha upaviśya sa vāsava sāmābhigāyati||

verses.meaning_en: Before beginning the morning chant, the sacrificer, sitting behind the Garhapatya Fire and facing the north, sings the Saman addressed to the Vasus: "O Fire! Open the door of the earthóworld. Let us see thee, that we may rule this earth.
verses.verse_skt: लो३कद्वारमपावा ३ र्णू ३३ पश्येम त्वा वयरा ३ ३ ३ ३ ३ हुं ३ आ ३३ ज्या ३ यो ३ आ ३२१११ इति॥
verses.verse_tl: lo3kadvāramapāvā 3 rṇū 33 paśyema tvā vayarā 3 3 3 3 3 huṁ 3 ā 33 jyā 3 yo 3 ā 32111 iti||

verses.meaning_en: Then the sacrificer offers an oblation, reciting thus: "Adoration to Agni, who dwells in the earthóworld! Secure this world for me, the sacrificer. That is the world for the sacrificer. "I, the sacrificer, will go thither when this life is over. Svaha!" Afterwards the sacrificer chants: "Cast away the bolt of the earthóworld." Having said this, he rises. To him the Vasus offer the world connected with the morning oblation.
verses.verse_skt: अथ जुहोति नमोऽग्नये पृथिवीक्षिते लोकक्षिते लोकं मे यजमानाय विन्दैष वै यजमानस्य लोक एतास्मि॥
verses.verse_tl: atha juhoti namo'gnaye pṛthivīkṣite lokakṣite lokaṁ me yajamānāya vindaiṣa vai yajamānasya loka etāsmi ||

verses.meaning_en: Then the sacrificer offers an oblation, reciting thus: "Adoration to Agni, who dwells in the earthóworld! Secure this world for me, the sacrificer. That is the world for the sacrificer. "I, the sacrificer, will go thither when this life is over. Svaha!" Afterwards the sacrificer chants: "Cast away the bolt of the earthóworld." Having said this, he rises. To him the Vasus offer the world connected with the morning oblation.
verses.verse_skt: अत्र यजमानः परस्तादायुषः स्वाहाऽपजहि परिघमित्युक्त्वोत्तिष्ठति तस्मै वसवः प्रातःसवन संप्रयच्छन्ति॥
verses.verse_tl: atra yajamānaḥ parastādāyuṣaḥ svāhā'pajahi parighamityuktvottiṣṭhati tasmai vasavaḥ prātaḥsavana saṁprayacchanti ||

verses.meaning_en: Before beginning the midday oblation, the sacrificer, sitting behind the Daxina Fire and facing the north, sings the Saman addressed to the Rudras: "O Fire! Open the door of the skyóworld. Let us see thee, that we may rule wide in the skyóworld."
verses.verse_skt: पुरा माध्यन्दिनस्य सवनस्योपाकरणाज्जघनेनाग्नीध्रीयस्योदङ्मुख उपविश्य स रौद्र सामाभिगायति॥
verses.verse_tl: purā mādhyandinasya savanasyopākaraṇājjaghanenāgnīdhrīyasyodaṅmukha upaviśya sa raudra sāmābhigāyati ||

verses.meaning_en: Before beginning the midday oblation, the sacrificer, sitting behind the Daxina Fire and facing the north, sings the Saman addressed to the Rudras: "O Fire! Open the door of the skyóworld. Let us see thee, that we may rule wide in the skyóworld."
verses.verse_skt: लो३कद्वारमपावा३ र्णू ३३ पश्येम त्वा वयं वैरा ३३३३३ हुं आ ३३ ज्या ३ यो ३ आ ३२१११ इति॥
verses.verse_tl: lo3kadvāramapāvā3 rṇū 33 paśyema tvā vayaṁ vairā 33333 huṁ ā 33 jyā 3 yo 3 ā 32111 iti ||

verses.meaning_en: Then the sacrificer offers an oblation, reciting thus: "Adoration to Vayu, who dwells in the skyóworld! Secure this world for me, the sacrificer. That is the world for the sacrificer. "I, the sacrificer, will go thither when this life is over. Svaha!" Afterwards the sacrificer chants: "Cast away the bolt of the skyóworld." Having said this, he rises. To him the Rudras offer the world connected with the midday oblation.
verses.verse_skt: अथ जुहोति नमो वायवेऽन्तरिक्षक्षिते लोकक्षिते लोकं मे यजमानाय विन्दैष वै यजमानस्य लोक एतास्मि॥
verses.verse_tl: atha juhoti namo vāyave'ntarikṣakṣite lokakṣite lokaṁ me yajamānāya vindaiṣa vai yajamānasya loka etāsmi ||

verses.meaning_en: Then the sacrificer offers an oblation, reciting thus: "Adoration to Vayu, who dwells in the skyóworld! Secure this world for me, the sacrificer. That is the world for the sacrificer. "I, the sacrificer, will go thither when this life is over. Svaha!" Afterwards the sacrificer chants: "Cast away the bolt of the skyóworld." Having said this, he rises. To him the Rudras offer the world connected with the midday oblation.
verses.verse_skt: अत्र यजमानः परस्तादायुषः स्वाहाऽपजहि परिघमित्युक्त्वोत्तिष्ठति तस्मै रुद्रा माध्यन्दिन सवन संप्रयच्छन्ति॥
verses.verse_tl: atra yajamānaḥ parastādāyuṣaḥ svāhā'pajahi parighamityuktvottiṣṭhati tasmai rudrā mādhyandina savana saṁprayacchanti||

verses.meaning_en: Before beginning the third (i.e. evening) oblation, the sacrificer, sitting behind the Ahavaniya Fire and facing the north, sings the two Samans addressed to the Adityas and the Visveó devas: "O Fire! Open the door of the heavenóworld. Let us see thee, that we may rule supreme in heaven." This is addressed to the Adityas. Next the Saman addressed to the Visveódevas: "O Fire! Open the door of the heavenóworld. Let us see thee, that we may rule supreme in heaven."
verses.verse_skt: पुरा तृतीयसवनस्योपाकरणाज्जघनेनाहवनीयस्योदङ्मुख उपविश्य स आदित्य स वैश्वदेव सामाभिगायति॥
verses.verse_tl: purā tṛtīyasavanasyopākaraṇājjaghanenāhavanīyasyodaṅmukha upaviśya sa āditya sa vaiśvadeva sāmābhigāyati||

verses.meaning_en: Before beginning the third (i.e. evening) oblation, the sacrificer, sitting behind the Ahavaniya Fire and facing the north, sings the two Samans addressed to the Adityas and the Visveó devas: "O Fire! Open the door of the heavenóworld. Let us see thee, that we may rule supreme in heaven." This is addressed to the Adityas. Next the Saman addressed to the Visveódevas: "O Fire! Open the door of the heavenóworld. Let us see thee, that we may rule supreme in heaven."
verses.verse_skt: लो३कद्वारमपा वा ३ र्णू ३३ पश्येम त्वा वय स्वारा ३३३३३ हुं ३ आ ३३ ज्या ३ यो ३ आ ३२१११ इति॥
verses.verse_tl: lo3kadvāramapā vā 3 rṇū 33 paśyema tvā vaya svārā 33333 huṁ 3 ā 33 jyā 3 yo 3 ā 32111 iti||

verses.meaning_en: Before beginning the third (i.e. evening) oblation, the sacrificer, sitting behind the Ahavaniya Fire and facing the north, sings the two Samans addressed to the Adityas and the Visveó devas: "O Fire! Open the door of the heavenóworld. Let us see thee, that we may rule supreme in heaven." This is addressed to the Adityas. Next the Saman addressed to the Visveódevas: "O Fire! Open the door of the heavenóworld. Let us see thee, that we may rule supreme in heaven."
verses.verse_skt: आदित्यमथ वैश्वदेवं लो३कद्वारमपावा ३ र्णू ३३ पश्येम त्वा वय साम्ना ३३३३३ हुं ३ आ ३३ ज्या ३ यो ३ आ ३२१११ इति॥
verses.verse_tl: ādityamatha vaiśvadevaṁ lo3kadvāramapāvā 3 rṇū 33 paśyema tvā vaya sāmnā 33333 huṁ 3 ā 33 jyā 3 yo 3 ā 32111 iti ||

verses.meaning_en: Then the sacrificer offers an oblation, reciting thus: "Adoration to Adityas and the Visveódevas, who dwell in the heavenó world! Secure this world for me, the sacrificer. That is the world for the sacrificer. "I, the sacrificer, will go thither when this life is over. Svaha! Afterwards the sacrificer chants: "Cast away the bolt of the heavenóworld." Having said this, he rises.
verses.verse_skt: अथ जुहोति नम आदित्येभ्यश्च विश्वेभ्यश्च देवेभ्यो दिविक्षिद्भ्यो लोकक्षिद्भ्यो लोकं मे यजमानाय विन्दत॥
verses.verse_tl: atha juhoti nama ādityebhyaśca viśvebhyaśca devebhyo divikṣidbhyo lokakṣidbhyo lokaṁ me yajamānāya vindata ||

verses.meaning_en: Then the sacrificer offers an oblation, reciting thus: "Adoration to Adityas and the Visveódevas, who dwell in the heavenó world! Secure this world for me, the sacrificer. That is the world for the sacrificer. "I, the sacrificer, will go thither when this life is over. Svaha! Afterwards the sacrificer chants: "Cast away the bolt of the heavenóworld." Having said this, he rises.
verses.verse_skt: एष वै यजमानस्य लोक एताऽस्म्यत्र यजमानः परस्तादायुषः स्वाहाऽपहत परिघमित्युक्त्वोत्तिष्ठति ॥
verses.verse_tl: eṣa vai yajamānasya loka etā'smyatra yajamānaḥ parastādāyuṣaḥ svāhā'pahata parighamityuktvottiṣṭhati ||

verses.meaning_en: To him the Adityas and the Visveódevas offer the world connected with the third oblation. He (the sacrificer) who knows this knows the measure of the sacrifice, yea, he knows it.
verses.verse_skt: तस्मा आदित्याश्च विश्वे च देवास्तृतीय सवन संप्रयच्छन्त्येष ह वै यज्ञस्य मात्रां वेद य एवं वेद य एवं वेद॥
verses.verse_tl: tasmā ādityāśca viśve ca devāstṛtīya savana saṁprayacchantyeṣa ha vai yajñasya mātrāṁ veda ya evaṁ veda ya evaṁ veda||

verses.meaning_en: Yonder sun is, verily, the honey of the gods. Heaven is the crossóbeam. The midóregion is the hive. The particles of wateróvapours drawn by the sun through its rays are the eggs.
verses.verse_skt: ॐ असौ वा आदित्यो देवमधु तस्य द्यौरेव तिरश्चीनवशोऽन्तरिक्षमपूपो मरीचयः पुत्राः॥
verses.verse_tl: om asau vā ādityo devamadhu tasya dyaureva tiraścīnavaśo'ntarikṣamapūpo marīcayaḥ putrāḥ||

verses.meaning_en: The eastern rays of the sun are the eastern honeyócells. The Rikóverses are the bees. The ritual laid down in the Rigó Veda is the flower. The water of the sacrificial libations is the nectar of the flower. These Riks heated the RigóVeda. From it, thus heated, issued forthóas its essenceófame, radiance of the body, vigour of the senses, virility and the food that is eaten.
verses.verse_skt: तस्य ये प्राञ्चो रश्मयस्ता एवास्य प्राच्यो मधुनाड्यः। ऋच एव मधुकृत ऋग्वेद एव पुष्पं ता अमृता आपस्ता वा एता ऋचः॥
verses.verse_tl: tasya ye prāñco raśmayastā evāsya prācyo madhunāḍyaḥ | ṛca eva madhukṛta ṛgveda eva puṣpaṁ tā amṛtā āpastā vā etā ṛcaḥ ||

verses.meaning_en: The eastern rays of the sun are the eastern honeyócells. The Rikóverses are the bees. The ritual laid down in the Rigó Veda is the flower. The water of the sacrificial libations is the nectar of the flower. These Riks heated the RigóVeda. From it, thus heated, issued forthóas its essenceófame, radiance of the body, vigour of the senses, virility and the food that is eaten.
verses.verse_skt: एतमृग्वेदमभ्यतपस्तस्याभितप्तस्य यशस्तेज इन्द्रियं वीर्यमन्नाद्य रसोऽजायत॥
verses.verse_tl: etamṛgvedamabhyatapastasyābhitaptasya yaśasteja indriyaṁ vīryamannādya raso'jāyata||

verses.meaning_en: That essence flowed forth and went toward the sun and that forms what is called the red colour of the rising sun.
verses.verse_skt: तद्व्यक्षरत्तदादित्यमभितोऽश्रयत्तद्वा एतद्यदेतदादित्यस्य रोहित रूपम्‌॥
verses.verse_tl: tadvyakṣarattadādityamabhito'śrayattadvā etadyadetadādityasya rohita rūpam||

verses.meaning_en: The southern rays of the sun are the southern honeyócells. The Yajusóverses are the bees. The ritual laid down in the Yajuró Veda is the flower. The water of the sacrificial libation is the nectar of the flower.
verses.verse_skt: अथ येऽस्य दक्षिणा रश्मयस्ता एवास्य दक्षिणा मधुनाड्यो यजूष्येव मधुकृतो यजुर्वेद एव पुष्पं ता अमृता आपः॥
verses.verse_tl: atha ye'sya dakṣiṇā raśmayastā evāsya dakṣiṇā madhunāḍyo yajūṣyeva madhukṛto yajurveda eva puṣpaṁ tā amṛtā āpaḥ ||

verses.meaning_en: These Yajusóverses heated the YajuróVeda. From it, thus heated, issued forthóas its essenceófame, radiance of the body, vigour of the senses, virility and the food that is eaten.
verses.verse_skt: तानि वा एतानि यजूष्येतं यजुर्वेदमभ्यतपस्तस्याभितप्तस्य यशस्तेज इन्द्रियं वीर्यमन्नाद्यरसोजायत॥
verses.verse_tl: tāni vā etāni yajūṣyetaṁ yajurvedamabhyatapastasyābhitaptasya yaśasteja indriyaṁ vīryamannādyarasojāyata||

verses.meaning_en: That essence flowed forth and went toward the sun. That forms what is called the white colour of the sun.
verses.verse_skt: तद्व्यक्षरत्तदादित्यमभितोऽश्रयत्तद्वा एतद्यदेतदादित्यस्य शुक्ल रूपम्‌॥
verses.verse_tl: tadvyakṣarattadādityamabhito'śrayattadvā etadyadetadādityasya śukla rūpam||

verses.meaning_en: The western rays of the sun are the western honeyócells. The Samanóverses are the bees. The SamaóVeda is the flower. The water is the nectar.
verses.verse_skt: अथ येऽस्य प्रत्यञ्चो रश्मयस्ता एवास्य प्रतीच्यो मधुनाड्यः सामान्येव मधुकृतः सामवेद एव पुष्पं ता अमृता आपः॥
verses.verse_tl: atha ye'sya pratyañco raśmayastā evāsya pratīcyo madhunāḍyaḥ sāmānyeva madhukṛtaḥ sāmaveda eva puṣpaṁ tā amṛtā āpaḥ ||

verses.meaning_en: The Samans heated the SamaóVeda. From it, thus heated, issued forthóas its essenceófame, radiance, vigour of the senses, virility and the food that is eaten.
verses.verse_skt: तानि वा एतानि सामान्येत सामवेदमभ्यतपस्तस्याभितप्तस्य यशस्तेज इन्द्रियं वीर्यमन्नाद्यरसोऽजायत॥
verses.verse_tl: tāni vā etāni sāmānyeta sāmavedamabhyatapastasyābhitaptasya yaśasteja indriyaṁ vīryamannādyaraso'jāyata||

verses.meaning_en: That flowed forth and went toward the sun. called the dark colour of the sun.
verses.verse_skt: तद्व्यक्षरत्तदादित्यमभितोऽश्रयत्तद्वा एतद्यदेतदादित्यस्य परं कृष्ण रूपम्‌॥
verses.verse_tl: tadvyakṣarattadādityamabhito'śrayattadvā etadyadetadādityasya paraṁ kṛṣṇa rūpam||

verses.meaning_en: The northern rays of the sun are the northern honeyócells. The verses of the Atharvangirasa are the bees. The Itihasaópurana is the flower. The water is the nectar.
verses.verse_skt: अथ येऽस्योदञ्चो रश्मयस्ता एवास्योदीच्यो मधुनाड्योऽथर्वाङ्गिरस एव मधुकृत इतिहासपुराणं पुष्पं ता अमृता आपः॥
verses.verse_tl: atha ye'syodañco raśmayastā evāsyodīcyo madhunāḍyo'tharvāṅgirasa eva madhukṛta itihāsapurāṇaṁ puṣpaṁ tā amṛtā āpaḥ ||

verses.meaning_en: These very hymns of the Atharvangirasa heated the Itihasaó purana. From it, thus heated, issued forthóas its essenceó fame, radiance, vigour of the senses, virility and the food that is eaten.
verses.verse_skt: ते वा एतेऽथर्वाङ्गिरस एतदितिहासपूराणमभ्यतप स्तस्याभितप्तस्य यशस्तेज इन्द्रियं वीर्यमन्नाद्य रसोऽजायत॥
verses.verse_tl: te vā ete'tharvāṅgirasa etaditihāsapūrāṇamabhyatapa stasyābhitaptasya yaśasteja indriyaṁ vīryamannādya raso'jāyata||

verses.meaning_en: That flowed forth and went toward the sun. That forms what is called the extremely dark colour of the sun.
verses.verse_skt: तद्व्यक्षरत्तदादित्यमभितोऽश्रयत्तद्वा एतद्यदेतदादित्यस्य परं कृष्ण रूपम्‌॥
verses.verse_tl: tadvyakṣarattadādityamabhito'śrayattadvā etadyadetadādityasya paraṁ kṛṣṇa rūpam ||

verses.meaning_en: Now, the upward rays of the sun are the honeyócells above. The secret teachings of the Upanishads are the bees. Brahman (Om) is flower. The water is the nectar.
verses.verse_skt: अथ येऽस्योर्ध्वा रश्मयस्ता एवास्योर्ध्वा मधुनाड्यो गुह्या एवादेशा मधुकृतो ब्रह्मैव पुष्पं ता अमृता आपः॥
verses.verse_tl: atha ye'syordhvā raśmayastā evāsyordhvā madhunāḍyo guhyā evādeśā madhukṛto brahmaiva puṣpaṁ tā amṛtā āpaḥ ||

verses.meaning_en: These secret teachings as the bees heated Brahman (Om). From It, thus heated, issued forthóas Its essenceófame, radiance, vigour of the senses, virility and the food that is eaten.
verses.verse_skt: ते वा एते गुह्या आदेशा एतद्ब्रह्माभ्यतपस्तस्याभितप्तस्य यशस्तेज इन्द्रियं वीर्यमन्नाद्य रसोऽजायत॥
verses.verse_tl: te vā ete guhyā ādeśā etadbrahmābhyatapastasyābhitaptasya yaśasteja indriyaṁ vīryamannādya raso'jāyata ||

verses.meaning_en: That flowed forth and went towards the sun. That forms what appears to stir in the centre of the sun.
verses.verse_skt: तद्व्यक्षरत्तदादित्यमभितोऽश्रयत्तद्वा एतद्यदेतदादित्यस्य मध्ये क्षोभत इव॥
verses.verse_tl: tadvyakṣarattadādityamabhito'śrayattadvā etadyadetadādityasya madhye kṣobhata iva ||

verses.meaning_en: These different colours in the sun are the essences of the essences; for the Vedas are the essences and these colours are, again, their essences. These are the nectars of the nectars; for the Vedas are the nectars (i.e. immortal) and of them these colours in the sun are the nectars.
verses.verse_skt: ते वा एते रसाना रसा वेदा हि रसास्तेषामेते रसास्तानि वा एतान्यमृतानाममृतानि वेदा ह्यमृतास्तेषामेतान्यमृतानि॥
verses.verse_tl: te vā ete rasānā rasā vedā hi rasāsteṣāmete rasāstāni vā etānyamṛtānāmamṛtāni vedā hyamṛtāsteṣāmetānyamṛtāni||

verses.meaning_en: On the first of these nectars the Vasus live, with Agni (fire) at their head. Truly, the gods do not eat or drink. They are satisfied by merely looking at the nectar.
verses.verse_skt: तद्यत्प्रथमममृतं तद्वसव उपजीवन्त्यग्निना मुखेन न वै देवा अश्नन्ति न पिबन्त्येतदेवामृतं दृष्ट्वा तृप्यन्ति॥
verses.verse_tl: tadyatprathamamamṛtaṁ tadvasava upajīvantyagninā mukhena na vai devā aśnanti na pibantyetadevāmṛtaṁ dṛṣṭvā tṛpyanti ||

verses.meaning_en: They retire into that red colour and rise up from that colour.
verses.verse_skt: त एतदेव रूपमभिसंविशन्त्येतस्माद्रूपादुद्यन्ति॥
verses.verse_tl: ta etadeva rūpamabhisaṁviśantyetasmādrūpādudyanti ||

verses.meaning_en: He who thus knows this nectar becomes one of the Vasus, with Agni (fire) at their head; he is satisfied by merely looking at the nectar. He retires into that red colour and again rises up from that colour.
verses.verse_skt: स य एतदेवममृतं वेद वसूनामेवैको भूत्वाऽग्निनैव मुखेनैतदेवामृतं दृष्ट्वा तृप्यति स य एतदेव रूपमभिसंविशत्येतस्माद्रूपादुदेति॥
verses.verse_tl: sa ya etadevamamṛtaṁ veda vasūnāmevaiko bhūtvā'gninaiva mukhenaitadevāmṛtaṁ dṛṣṭvā tṛpyati sa ya etadeva rūpamabhisaṁviśatyetasmādrūpādudeti ||

verses.meaning_en: As long as the sun rises in the east and sets in the west, so long does he, like the Vasus, enjoy rulership and sovereignty.
verses.verse_skt: स यावदादित्यः पुरस्तादुदेता पश्चादस्तमेता वसूनामेव तावदाधिपत्य स्वाराज्यं पर्येता॥
verses.verse_tl: sa yāvadādityaḥ purastādudetā paścādastametā vasūnāmeva tāvadādhipatya svārājyaṁ paryetā||

verses.meaning_en: On the second of these nectars the Rudras live, with Indra at their head. Truly, the gods do not eat or drink. They are satisfied by merely looking at the nectar.
verses.verse_skt: अथ यद्वितीयममृतं तद्रुद्रा उपजीवन्तीन्द्रेण मुखेन न वै देवा अश्नन्ति न पिबन्त्येतदेवामृतं दृष्ट्वा तृप्यन्ति॥
verses.verse_tl: atha yadvitīyamamṛtaṁ tadrudrā upajīvantīndreṇa mukhena na vai devā aśnanti na pibantyetadevāmṛtaṁ dṛṣṭvā tṛpyanti ||

verses.meaning_en: They retire into that white colour and rise up from that colour.
verses.verse_skt: त एतदेव रूपमभिसंविशन्त्येतस्माद्रूपादुद्यन्ति॥
verses.verse_tl: ta etadeva rūpamabhisaṁviśantyetasmādrūpādudyanti ||

verses.meaning_en: He who thus knows this nectar becomes one of the Rudras, with Indra at their head; he is satisfied by merely looking at the nectar. He retires into that white colour and again rises up from that colour.
verses.verse_skt: स य एतदेवममृतं वेद रुद्राणामेवैको भूत्वेन्द्रेणैव मुखेनैतदेवामृतं दृष्ट्वा तृप्यति स एतदेव रूपमभिसंविशत्येतस्माद्रूपादुदेति॥
verses.verse_tl: sa ya etadevamamṛtaṁ veda rudrāṇāmevaiko bhūtvendreṇaiva mukhenaitadevāmṛtaṁ dṛṣṭvā tṛpyati sa etadeva rūpamabhisaṁviśatyetasmādrūpādudeti ||

verses.meaning_en: As long as the sun rises in the east and sets in the west, twice as long does it rise in the south and set in the north and just so long does he, like the Rudras, enjoy rulership and sovereignty.
verses.verse_skt: स यावदादित्यः पुरस्तादुदेता पश्चादस्तमेता द्विस्तावद्दक्षिणत उदेतोत्तरतोऽस्तमेता रुद्राणामेव तावदाधिपत्य स्वाराज्यं पर्येता॥
verses.verse_tl: sa yāvadādityaḥ purastādudetā paścādastametā dvistāvaddakṣiṇata udetottarato'stametā rudrāṇāmeva tāvadādhipatya svārājyaṁ paryetā||

verses.meaning_en: On the third of these nectars the Adityas live, with Varuna at their head. Truly, the gods do not eat or drink. They are satisfied by merely looking at the nectar.
verses.verse_skt: अथ यत्तृतीयममृतं तदादित्या उपजीवन्ति वरुणेन मुखेन न वै देवा अश्नन्ति न पिबन्त्येतदेवामृतं दृष्ट्वा तृप्यन्ति॥
verses.verse_tl: atha yattṛtīyamamṛtaṁ tadādityā upajīvanti varuṇena mukhena na vai devā aśnanti na pibantyetadevāmṛtaṁ dṛṣṭvā tṛpyanti ||

verses.meaning_en: They retire into that dark colour and rise up from that colour.
verses.verse_skt: त एतदेव रूपमभिसंविशन्त्येतस्माद्रूपादुद्यन्ति॥
verses.verse_tl: ta etadeva rūpamabhisaṁviśantyetasmādrūpādudyanti ||

verses.meaning_en: He who thus knows this nectar becomes one of the Adityas, with Varuna at their head; he is satisfied by merely looking at the nectar. He returns into that dark colour and again rises up from that colour.
verses.verse_skt: स य एतदेवममृतं वेदादित्यानामेवैको भूत्वा वरुणेनैव मुखेनैतदेवामृतं दृष्ट्वा तृप्यति स एतदेव रूपमभिसंविशत्येतस्माद्रूपादुदेति॥
verses.verse_tl: sa ya etadevamamṛtaṁ vedādityānāmevaiko bhūtvā varuṇenaiva mukhenaitadevāmṛtaṁ dṛṣṭvā tṛpyati sa etadeva rūpamabhisaṁviśatyetasmādrūpādudeti ||

verses.meaning_en: As long as the sun rises in the south and sets in the north, twice as long does it rise in the west and set in the east and just so long does he, like the Adityas, enjoy rulership and sovereignty.
verses.verse_skt: स यावदादित्यो दक्षिणत उदेतोत्तरतोऽस्तमेता द्विस्तावत्पश्चादुदेता पुरस्तादस्तमेताऽऽदित्यानामेव तावदाधिपत्य स्वाराज्यं पर्येता॥
verses.verse_tl: sa yāvadādityo dakṣiṇata udetottarato'stametā dvistāvatpaścādudetā purastādastametā''dityānāmeva tāvadādhipatya svārājyaṁ paryetā||

verses.meaning_en: On the fourth of these nectars the Maruts live, with Soma at their head. Truly, the gods do not eat or drink. They are satisfied by merely looking at the nectar.
verses.verse_skt: अथ यच्चतुर्थममृतं तन्मरुत उपजीवन्ति सोमेन मुखेन न वै देवा अश्नन्ति न पिबन्त्येतदेवामृतं दृष्ट्वा तृप्यन्ति॥
verses.verse_tl: atha yaccaturthamamṛtaṁ tanmaruta upajīvanti somena mukhena na vai devā aśnanti na pibantyetadevāmṛtaṁ dṛṣṭvā tṛpyanti ||

verses.meaning_en: They retire into that extremely dark colour and rise up from that colour.
verses.verse_skt: त एतदेव रूपमभिसंविशन्त्येतस्माद्रूपादुद्यन्ति॥
verses.verse_tl: ta etadeva rūpamabhisaṁviśantyetasmādrūpādudyanti ||

verses.meaning_en: He who thus knows this nectar becomes one of the Maruts, with Soma at their head; he is satisfied by merely looking at the nectar. He retires into that extremely dark colour and again rises up from that colour.
verses.verse_skt: स य एतदेवममृतं वेद मरुतामेवैको भूत्वा सोमेनैव मुखेनैतदेवामृतं दृष्ट्वा तृप्यति स एतदेव रूपमभिसंविशत्येतस्माद्रूपादुदेति॥
verses.verse_tl: sa ya etadevamamṛtaṁ veda marutāmevaiko bhūtvā somenaiva mukhenaitadevāmṛtaṁ dṛṣṭvā tṛpyati sa etadeva rūpamabhisaṁviśatyetasmādrūpādudeti ||

verses.meaning_en: As long as the sun rises in the west and sets in the east, twice as long does it rise in the north and set in the south and just so long does he, like the Maruts, enjoy rulership and sovereignty.
verses.verse_skt: स यावदादित्यः पश्चादुदेता पुरस्तादस्तमेता द्विस्तावदुत्तरत उदेता दक्षिणतोऽस्तमेता मरुतामेव तावदाधिपत्य स्वाराज्यं पर्येता॥
verses.verse_tl: sa yāvadādityaḥ paścādudetā purastādastametā dvistāvaduttarata udetā dakṣiṇato'stametā marutāmeva tāvadādhipatya svārājyaṁ paryetā||

verses.meaning_en: On the fifth of these nectars the Sadhyas live, with Brahma at their head. Truly, the gods do not eat or drink. They are satisfied by merely looking at the nectar.
verses.verse_skt: अथ यत्पञ्चमममृतं तत्साध्या उपजीवन्ति ब्रह्मणा मुखेन न वै देवा अश्नन्ति न पिबन्त्येतदेवामृतं दृष्ट्वा तृप्यन्ति॥
verses.verse_tl: atha yatpañcamamamṛtaṁ tatsādhyā upajīvanti brahmaṇā mukhena na vai devā aśnanti na pibantyetadevāmṛtaṁ dṛṣṭvā tṛpyanti ||

verses.meaning_en: Thy retire into that form and rise up from that form.
verses.verse_skt: त एतदेव रूपमभिसंविशन्त्येतस्माद्रूपादुद्यन्ति॥
verses.verse_tl: ta etadeva rūpamabhisaṁviśantyetasmādrūpādudyanti ||

verses.meaning_en: He who knows this nectar becomes one of the Sadhyas, with Brahma at their head; he is satisfied by merely looking at the nectar. He retires into that form and again rises up from that form.
verses.verse_skt: स य एतदेवममृतं वेद साध्यानामेवैको भूत्वा ब्रह्मणैव मुखेनैतदेवामृतं दृष्ट्वा तृप्यति स एतदेव रूपमभिसंविशत्येतस्माद्रूपादुदेति॥
verses.verse_tl: sa ya etadevamamṛtaṁ veda sādhyānāmevaiko bhūtvā brahmaṇaiva mukhenaitadevāmṛtaṁ dṛṣṭvā tṛpyati sa etadeva rūpamabhisaṁviśatyetasmādrūpādudeti ||

verses.meaning_en: As long as the sun rises in the north and sets in the south, twice as long does it rise above and set below and just so long does he, like the Sadhyas, enjoy rulership and sovereignty.
verses.verse_skt: स यावदादित्य उत्तरत उदेता दक्षिणतोऽस्तमेता द्विस्तावदूर्ध्वं उदेताऽर्वागस्तमेता साध्यानामेव तावदाधिपत्य स्वाराज्यं पर्येता॥
verses.verse_tl: sa yāvadāditya uttarata udetā dakṣiṇato'stametā dvistāvadūrdhvaṁ udetā'rvāgastametā sādhyānāmeva tāvadādhipatya svārājyaṁ paryetā ||

verses.meaning_en: Now, after having risen thence upwards, it (i.e. the sun) rises and sets no more. It remains alone in the centre. And on this there is the following verse:
verses.verse_skt: अथ तत ऊर्ध्व उदेत्य नैवोदेता नास्तमेतैकल एव मध्ये स्थाता तदेष श्लोकः॥
verses.verse_tl: atha tata ūrdhva udetya naivodetā nāstametaikala eva madhye sthātā tadeṣa ślokaḥ ||

verses.meaning_en: "There (i.e. in Brahmaloka) the sun neither rises nor sets at any time. O ye gods, if this is true, may I never fall from Brahman!"
verses.verse_skt: न वै तत्र न निम्लोच नोदियाय कदाचन। देवास्तेनाह सत्येन मा विराधिषि ब्रह्मणेति॥
verses.verse_tl: na vai tatra na nimloca nodiyāya kadācana | devāstenāha satyena mā virādhiṣi brahmaṇeti ||

verses.meaning_en: Verily, for him who thus knows this BrahmaóUpanishad, the sun does not rise or set. For him it is day for ever.
verses.verse_skt: न ह वा अस्मा उदेति न निम्लोचति सकृद्दिवा हैवास्मै भवति य एतामेवं ब्रह्मोपनिषदं वेद॥
verses.verse_tl: na ha vā asmā udeti na nimlocati sakṛddivā haivāsmai bhavati ya etāmevaṁ brahmopaniṣadaṁ veda ||

verses.meaning_en: This doctrine Brahma told to Prajapati, Prajapati to Manu, Manu to his offspring. And to Uddalaka Aruni this doctrine of Brahman was narrated by his father.
verses.verse_skt: तद्धैतद्ब्रह्मा प्रजापतय उवाच प्रजापतिर्मनवे मनुः प्रजाभ्यस्तद्धैतदुद्दालकायारुणये ज्येष्ठाय पुत्राय पिता ब्रह्म प्रोवाच॥
verses.verse_tl: taddhaitadbrahmā prajāpataya uvāca prajāpatirmanave manuḥ prajābhyastaddhaitaduddālakāyāruṇaye jyeṣṭhāya putrāya pitā brahma provāca ||

verses.meaning_en: A father may therefore tell that doctrine of Brahman to his eldest son to a worthy disciple.
verses.verse_skt: इदं वाव तज्ज्येष्ठाय पुत्राय पिता ब्रह्म प्रब्रूयात्प्रणाय्याय वाऽन्तेवासिने॥
verses.verse_tl: idaṁ vāva tajjyeṣṭhāya putrāya pitā brahma prabrūyātpraṇāyyāya vā'ntevāsine ||

verses.meaning_en: It must not be told to anyone else, even if he should offer one the whole seaógirt earth, full of treasure; for this doctrine is worth more an that, yea, it is worth more.
verses.verse_skt: नान्यस्मै कस्मैचन यद्यप्यस्मा इमामद्भिः परिगृहीतां धनस्य पूर्णां दद्यादेतदेव ततो भूय इत्येतदेव ततो भूय इति॥
verses.verse_tl: nānyasmai kasmaicana yadyapyasmā imāmadbhiḥ parigṛhītāṁ dhanasya pūrṇāṁ dadyādetadeva tato bhūya ityetadeva tato bhūya iti ||

verses.meaning_en: The gayatri is everything, whatever here exists. Speech is verily the Gayatri, for speech sings forth (gayaóti) and protects (trayaóte) everything, whatever here exists.
verses.verse_skt: गायत्री वा ईद सर्वं भूतं यदिदं किंच वाग्वै गायत्री वाग्वा इद सर्वं भूतं गायति च त्रायते च॥
verses.verse_tl: gāyatrī vā īda sarvaṁ bhūtaṁ yadidaṁ kiṁca vāgvai gāyatrī vāgvā ida sarvaṁ bhūtaṁ gāyati ca trāyate ca||

verses.meaning_en: That Gayatri is also the earth; for everything that exists here rests on this earth and does not go beyond.
verses.verse_skt: या वै सा गायत्रीयं वाव सा येयं पृथिव्यस्या हीद सर्वं भूतं प्रतिष्ठितमेतामेव नातिशीयते॥
verses.verse_tl: yā vai sā gāyatrīyaṁ vāva sā yeyaṁ pṛthivyasyā hīda sarvaṁ bhūtaṁ pratiṣṭhitametāmeva nātiśīyate||

verses.meaning_en: In man, that Gayatri is also the body; for the pranas exist in this body and do not go beyond.
verses.verse_skt: या वै सा पृथिवीयं वाव सा यदिदमस्मिन्पुरुषे शरीरमस्मिन्हीमे प्राणाः प्रतिष्ठिता एतदेव नातिशीयन्ते॥
verses.verse_tl: yā vai sā pṛthivīyaṁ vāva sā yadidamasminpuruṣe śarīramasminhīme prāṇāḥ pratiṣṭhitā etadeva nātiśīyante ||

verses.meaning_en: That body, in man, is again the heart within a man; for the pranas exist in it and do not go beyond.
verses.verse_skt: यद्वै तत्पुरुषे शरीरमिदं वाव तद्यदिदमस्मिन्नन्तः पुरुषे हृदयमस्मिन्हीमे प्राणाः प्रतिष्ठिता एतदेव नातिशीयन्ते॥
verses.verse_tl: yadvai tatpuruṣe śarīramidaṁ vāva tadyadidamasminnantaḥ puruṣe hṛdayamasminhīme prāṇāḥ pratiṣṭhitā etadeva nātiśīyante ||

verses.meaning_en: That Gayatri has four feet and is sixfold. The same is also declared by a Rikóverse:
verses.verse_skt: सैषा चतुष्पदा षड्विधा गायत्री तदेतदृचाभ्यनूक्तम्‌॥
verses.verse_tl: saiṣā catuṣpadā ṣaḍvidhā gāyatrī tadetadṛcābhyanūktam ||

verses.meaning_en: "Such is its greatness (i.e. of Brahman as known through the symbol of the Gayatri). Greater than it is the Person (Brahman). One of Its feet covers all beings; the immortal three feet are in heaven (i.e. in Itself)
verses.verse_skt: तावानस्य महिमा ततो ज्यायाश्च पूरुषः। पादोऽस्य सर्वा भूतानि त्रिपादस्यामृतं दिवीति॥
verses.verse_tl: tāvānasya mahimā tato jyāyāśca pūruṣaḥ | pādo'sya sarvā bhūtāni tripādasyāmṛtaṁ divīti ||

verses.meaning_en: The Brahman which has been thus described is the same as the physical akasa outside a person. The akasa which is outside a person is the same as that which is inside a person. The akasa which is inside a person is the akasa within the heart. The akasa which is within the heart is omnipresent and unchanging. He who knows this obtains full and unchanging prosperity.
verses.verse_skt: यद्वै तद्ब्रह्मेतीदं वाव तद्योऽयं बहिर्धा पुरुषादाकाशो यो वै स बहिर्धा पुरुषादाकाशः॥
verses.verse_tl: yadvai tadbrahmetīdaṁ vāva tadyo'yaṁ bahirdhā puruṣādākāśo yo vai sa bahirdhā puruṣādākāśaḥ ||

verses.meaning_en: The Brahman which has been thus described is the same as the physical akasa outside a person. The akasa which is outside a person is the same as that which is inside a person. The akasa which is inside a person is the akasa within the heart. The akasa which is within the heart is omnipresent and unchanging. He who knows this obtains full and unchanging prosperity.
verses.verse_skt: अयं वाव स योऽयमन्तः पुरुष अकाशो यो वै सोऽन्तः पुरुष आकाशः॥
verses.verse_tl: ayaṁ vāva sa yo'yamantaḥ puruṣa akāśo yo vai so'ntaḥ puruṣa ākāśaḥ ||

verses.meaning_en: The Brahman which has been thus described is the same as the physical akasa outside a person. The akasa which is outside a person is the same as that which is inside a person. The akasa which is inside a person is the akasa within the heart. The akasa which is within the heart is omnipresent and unchanging. He who knows this obtains full and unchanging prosperity.
verses.verse_skt: अयं वाव स योऽयमन्तर्हृदय आकाशस्तदेतत्पूर्णमप्रवर्ति पूर्णमप्रवर्तिनी श्रियं लभते य एवं वेद॥
verses.verse_tl: ayaṁ vāva sa yo'yamantarhṛdaya ākāśastadetatpūrṇamapravarti pūrṇamapravartinī śriyaṁ labhate ya evaṁ veda ||

verses.meaning_en: Of that heart there are five doors controlled by the devas. That which is the eastern door is the pranaóthat is the eye, that is Aditya (the sun). One should meditate on that as brightness and the source of food. He who knows this becomes bright and an eater of food.
verses.verse_skt: तस्य ह वा एतस्य हृदयस्य पञ्च देवसुषयः स योऽस्य प्राङ्सुषिः स प्राणस्तच्चक्षुः स आदित्यस्तदेतत्तेजोऽन्नाद्यमित्युपासीत तेजस्व्यन्नादो भवति य एवं वेद॥
verses.verse_tl: tasya ha vā etasya hṛdayasya pañca devasuṣayaḥ sa yo'sya prāṅsuṣiḥ sa prāṇastaccakṣuḥ sa ādityastadetattejo'nnādyamityupāsīta tejasvyannādo bhavati ya evaṁ veda ||

verses.meaning_en: That which is the southern gate is the vyanaóthat is the ear, that is Chandrama (the moon). One should meditate on that as prosperity and fame. He who knows this becomes prosperous and famous.
verses.verse_skt: अथ योऽस्य दक्षिणः सुषिः स व्यानस्तच्छ्रोत्र स चन्द्रमास्तदेतच्छ्रीश्च यशश्चेत्युपासीत श्रीमान्यशस्वी भवति य एवं वेद॥
verses.verse_tl: atha yo'sya dakṣiṇaḥ suṣiḥ sa vyānastacchrotra sa candramāstadetacchrīśca yaśaścetyupāsīta śrīmānyaśasvī bhavati ya evaṁ veda ||

verses.meaning_en: That which is the western gate is the apanaóthat is speech, that is Agni (fire). One should meditate on that as the radiance of Brahman and the source of food. He who knows this becomes radiant and an eater of food.
verses.verse_skt: अथ योऽस्य प्रत्यङ्सुषिः सोऽपानः सा वाक्सोऽग्निस्तदेतद्ब्रह्मवर्चसमन्नाद्यमित्युपासीत ब्रह्मवर्चस्यन्नादो भवति य एवं वेद॥
verses.verse_tl: atha yo'sya pratyaṅsuṣiḥ so'pānaḥ sā vākso'gnistadetadbrahmavarcasamannādyamityupāsīta brahmavarcasyannādo bhavati ya evaṁ veda ||

verses.meaning_en: That which is the northern gate is the samanaóthat is the mind, that is Parjanya (the rainógod). One should meditate on that as fame and beauty. He who knows this becomes famous and beautiful.
verses.verse_skt: अथ योऽस्योदङ्सुषिः स समानस्तन्मनः स पर्जन्यस्तदेतत्कीर्तिश्च व्युष्टिश्चेत्युपासीत कीर्तिमान्व्युष्टिमान्भवति य एवं वेद॥
verses.verse_tl: atha yo'syodaṅsuṣiḥ sa samānastanmanaḥ sa parjanyastadetatkīrtiśca vyuṣṭiścetyupāsīta kīrtimānvyuṣṭimānbhavati ya evaṁ veda ||

verses.meaning_en: That which is the upper gate is the udanaóthat is Vayu, that is the akasa. One should meditate on that as strength and greatness. He who knows this becomes strong and great.
verses.verse_skt: अथ योऽस्योर्ध्वः सुषिः स उदानः स वायुः स आकाशस्तदेतदोजश्च महश्चेत्युपासीतौजस्वी महस्वान्भवति य एवं वेद॥
verses.verse_tl: atha yo'syordhvaḥ suṣiḥ sa udānaḥ sa vāyuḥ sa ākāśastadetadojaśca mahaścetyupāsītaujasvī mahasvānbhavati ya evaṁ veda ||

verses.meaning_en: These are the five servants of Brahman, the doorókeepers of the world of heaven. He who thus knows these five servants of Brahman, the doorókeepers of the world of heavenóin his family a hero is born. He who thus knows the five servants of Brahman, the doorókeepers of the world of heaven, himself attains the world of heaven.
verses.verse_skt: ते वा एते पञ्च ब्रह्मपुरुषाः स्वर्गस्य लोकस्य द्वारपाः स य एतानेवं पञ्च ब्रह्मपुरुषान्स्वर्गस्य लोकस्य द्वारपान्वेदास्य कुले वीरो जायते प्रतिपद्यते स्वर्गं लोकं य एतानेवं पञ्च ब्रह्मपुरुषान्स्वर्गस्य लोकस्य द्वारपान्वेद॥
verses.verse_tl: te vā ete pañca brahmapuruṣāḥ svargasya lokasya dvārapāḥ sa ya etānevaṁ pañca brahmapuruṣānsvargasya lokasya dvārapānvedāsya kule vīro jāyate pratipadyate svargaṁ lokaṁ ya etānevaṁ pañca brahmapuruṣānsvargasya lokasya dvārapānveda ||

verses.meaning_en: Now, the light which shines above this heaven, above all the worlds, above everything, in the highest worlds not excelled by any other worlds, that is the same light which is within man. There is this visible of this light: when we thus perceive by touch the warmth in the body. And of it we have this audible proof: when we thus hear, by covering the ears, what is like the rumbling of a carriage, or the bellowing of an ox, or the sound of a blazing fire. One should worship as Brahman that inner light which is seen and heard. He who knows becomes conspicuous and celebrated, yea, he becomes celebrated.
verses.verse_skt: अथ यदतः परो दिवो ज्योतिर्दीप्यते विश्वतः पृष्ठेषु सर्वतः पृष्ठेष्वनुत्तमेषूत्तमेषु लोकेष्विदं वाव तद्यदिदमस्मिन्नन्तः पुरुषे ज्योतिः तस्यैषा दृष्टिर्यत्रितदस्मिञ्छरीरे सस्पर्शेनोष्णिमानं विजानाति तस्यैषा श्रुतिर्यत्रैतत्कर्णावपिगृह्य निनदमिव नदथुरिवाग्नेरिव ज्वलत उपशृणोति तदेतद्दृष्टं च श्रुतं चेत्युपासीत चक्षुष्यः श्रुतो भवति य एवं वेद य एवं वेद॥
verses.verse_tl: atha yadataḥ paro divo jyotirdīpyate viśvataḥ pṛṣṭheṣu sarvataḥ pṛṣṭheṣvanuttameṣūttameṣu lokeṣvidaṁ vāva tadyadidamasminnantaḥ puruṣe jyotiḥ tasyaiṣā dṛṣṭiryatritadasmiñcharīre sasparśenoṣṇimānaṁ vijānāti tasyaiṣā śrutiryatraitatkarṇāvapigṛhya ninadamiva nadathurivāgneriva jvalata upaśṛṇoti tadetaddṛṣṭaṁ ca śrutaṁ cetyupāsīta cakṣuṣyaḥ śruto bhavati ya evaṁ veda ya evaṁ veda||

verses.meaning_en: All this is Brahman. From It the universe comes forth, in It the universe merges and in It the universe breathes. Therefore a man should meditate on Brahman with a calm mind. Now, verily, a man consists of will. As he wills in this world, so does he become when he has departed hence. Let him with this knowledge in mind form his will.
verses.verse_skt: सर्वं खल्विदं ब्रह्म तज्जलानिति शान्त उपासीत। अथ खलु क्रतुमयः पुरुषो यथाक्रतुरस्मिल्लोके पुरुषो भवति तथेतः प्रेत्य भवति स क्रतुं कुर्वीत॥
verses.verse_tl: sarvaṁ khalvidaṁ brahma tajjalāniti śānta upāsīta | atha khalu kratumayaḥ puruṣo yathākraturasmilloke puruṣo bhavati tathetaḥ pretya bhavati sa kratuṁ kurvīta ||

verses.meaning_en: He who consists of the mind, whose body is subtle, whose form is light, whose thoughts are true, whose nature is like the akasa, whose creation in this universe, who cherishes all righteous desires, who contains all pleasant odours, who is endowed with all tastes, who embraces all this, who never speaks and who is without longingó He is my Self within the heart, smaller than a grain of rice, smaller than a grain of barley, smaller than a mustard seed, smaller than a grain of millet; He is my Self within the heart, greater than the earth, greater than the midóregion, greater than heaven, greater than all these worlds.
verses.verse_skt: मनोमयः प्राणशरीरो भारूपः सत्यसंकल्प आकाशात्मा सर्वकर्मा सर्वकामः सर्वगन्धः सर्वरसः सर्वमिदमभ्यत्तोऽवाक्यनादरः॥
verses.verse_tl: manomayaḥ prāṇaśarīro bhārūpaḥ satyasaṁkalpa ākāśātmā sarvakarmā sarvakāmaḥ sarvagandhaḥ sarvarasaḥ sarvamidamabhyatto'vākyanādaraḥ ||

verses.meaning_en: He who consists of the mind, whose body is subtle, whose form is light, whose thoughts are true, whose nature is like the akasa, whose creation in this universe, who cherishes all righteous desires, who contains all pleasant odours, who is endowed with all tastes, who embraces all this, who never speaks and who is without longingó He is my Self within the heart, smaller than a grain of rice, smaller than a grain of barley, smaller than a mustard seed, smaller than a grain of millet; He is my Self within the heart, greater than the earth, greater than the midóregion, greater than heaven, greater than all these worlds.
verses.verse_skt: एष म आत्माऽन्तर्हृदयेऽणीयान्व्रीहेर्वा यवाद्वा सर्षपाद्वा श्यामाकाद्वा श्यामाकतण्डुलाद्वा एष म आत्माऽन्तर्हृदये ज्यायान्पृथिव्या ज्यायानन्तरिक्षाज्ज्यायान्दिवो ज्यायानेभ्यो लोकेभ्यः॥
verses.verse_tl: eṣa ma ātmā'ntarhṛdaye'ṇīyānvrīhervā yavādvā sarṣapādvā śyāmākādvā śyāmākataṇḍulādvā eṣa ma ātmā'ntarhṛdaye jyāyānpṛthivyā jyāyānantarikṣājjyāyāndivo jyāyānebhyo lokebhyaḥ ||

verses.meaning_en: He whose creation is this universe, who cherishes all desires, who contains all odours, who is endowed with all tastes, who embraces all this, who never speaks and who is without longingóHe is my Self within the heart, He is that Brahman. When I shall have departed hence I shall certainly reach Him: one who has this faith and has no doubt will certainly attain to that Godhead. Thus said Sandilya, yea, thus he said.
verses.verse_skt: सर्वकर्मा सर्वकामः सर्वगन्धः सर्वरसः सर्वमिदमभ्यात्तोऽवाक्यनादर एष म आत्माऽन्तर्हृदय एतद्ब्रह्मैतमितः प्रेत्याभिसंभवितास्मीति यस्य स्यादद्धा न विचिकित्साऽस्तीति ह स्माह शाण्डिल्यः शाण्डिल्यः ॥
verses.verse_tl: sarvakarmā sarvakāmaḥ sarvagandhaḥ sarvarasaḥ sarvamidamabhyātto'vākyanādara eṣa ma ātmā'ntarhṛdaya etadbrahmaitamitaḥ pretyābhisaṁbhavitāsmīti yasya syādaddhā na vicikitsā'stīti ha smāha śāṇḍilyaḥ śāṇḍilyaḥ ||

verses.meaning_en: The chest of the universe, with the midóregion for its inside and the earth for its bottom, does not decay. The quarters are its different corners and heaven is its lid, which is above. This chest is the storehouse of treasures. Inside it are all things.
verses.verse_skt: अन्तरिक्षोदरः कोशो भूमिबुध्नो न जीर्यति दिशो ह्यस्य स्रक्तयो द्यौरस्योत्तरं बिल स एष कोशो वसुधानस्तस्मिन्विश्वमिद श्रितम्‌॥
verses.verse_tl: antarikṣodaraḥ kośo bhūmibudhno na jīryati diśo hyasya sraktayo dyaurasyottaraṁ bila sa eṣa kośo vasudhānastasminviśvamida śritam||

verses.meaning_en: The eastern quarter is called Juhu, the southern quarter Sahamana, the western quarter Rajni and the northern quarter Subhuta. Vayu the air is their child. He who knows this Vayu as the child of the quarters never weeps for his sons. I know the air to be the child of the quarters; may I never weep for my sons.
verses.verse_skt: तस्य प्राची दिग्जुहूर्नाम सहमाना नाम दक्षिणा राज्ञी नाम प्रतीची सुभूता नामोदीची तासां वायुर्वत्सः स य एतमेवं वायुं दिशां वत्सं वेद न पुत्ररोद रोदिति सोऽहमेतमेवं वायुं दिशां वत्सं वेद मा पुत्ररोद रुदम्‌॥
verses.verse_tl: tasya prācī digjuhūrnāma sahamānā nāma dakṣiṇā rājñī nāma pratīcī subhūtā nāmodīcī tāsāṁ vāyurvatsaḥ sa ya etamevaṁ vāyuṁ diśāṁ vatsaṁ veda na putraroda roditi so'hametamevaṁ vāyuṁ diśāṁ vatsaṁ veda mā putraroda rudam||

verses.meaning_en: I take refuge in the imperishable chest with this one and this one and this one. I take refuge in the prana with this one and this one and this one. I take refuge in Bhuh with this one and this one and this one. I take refuge in Bhuvah with this one and this one and this one. I take refuge Svah with this one and this one and this one.
verses.verse_skt: अरिष्टं कोशं प्रपद्येऽमुनाऽमुनाऽमुना प्राणं प्रपद्येऽमुनाऽमुनाऽमुना भूः प्रपद्येऽमुनाऽमुनाऽमुना भुवः प्रपद्येऽमुनाऽमुनाऽमुना स्वः प्रपद्येऽमुनाऽमुनाऽमुना॥
verses.verse_tl: ariṣṭaṁ kośaṁ prapadye'munā'munā'munā prāṇaṁ prapadye'munā'munā'munā bhūḥ prapadye'munā'munā'munā bhuvaḥ prapadye'munā'munā'munā svaḥ prapadye'munā'munā'munā ||

verses.meaning_en: When I said: "I take refuge in the prana," prana meant everything that exists hereóin that I take refuge.
verses.verse_skt: स यदवोचं प्राणं प्रपद्य इति प्राणो वा इद सर्वं भूतं यदिदं किंच तमेव तत्प्रापत्सि॥
verses.verse_tl: sa yadavocaṁ prāṇaṁ prapadya iti prāṇo vā ida sarvaṁ bhūtaṁ yadidaṁ kiṁca tameva tatprāpatsi||

verses.meaning_en: When I said: "I take refuge in Bhuh," what I really said was: "I refuge in the earth, the midóregion and heaven."
verses.verse_skt: अथ यदवोचं भूः प्रपद्य इति पृथिवीं प्रपद्येऽन्तरिक्षं प्रपद्ये दिवं प्रपद्य इत्येव तदवोचम्‌॥
verses.verse_tl: atha yadavocaṁ bhūḥ prapadya iti pṛthivīṁ prapadye'ntarikṣaṁ prapadye divaṁ prapadya ityeva tadavocam ||

verses.meaning_en: Then I said: "I take refuge in Bhuvah," what I said was: "I take in fire, the air and the sun."
verses.verse_skt: अथ यदवोचं भुवः प्रपद्य इत्यग्निं प्रपद्ये वायुं प्रपद्य आदित्यं प्रपद्य इत्येव तदवोचम्‌॥
verses.verse_tl: atha yadavocaṁ bhuvaḥ prapadya ityagniṁ prapadye vāyuṁ prapadya ādityaṁ prapadya ityeva tadavocam ||

verses.meaning_en: When I said: "I take refuge in Svah," what I said was: "I take refuge in the RigóVeda, YajuróVeda and SamaóVeda." That is what I said, yea, that is what I said.
verses.verse_skt: अथ यदवोच स्वः प्रपद्य इत्यृग्वेदं प्रपद्ये यजुर्वेदं प्रपद्ये सामवेदं प्रपद्य इत्येव तदवोचं तदवोचम्‌॥
verses.verse_tl: atha yadavoca svaḥ prapadya ityṛgvedaṁ prapadye yajurvedaṁ prapadye sāmavedaṁ prapadya ityeva tadavocaṁ tadavocam||

verses.meaning_en: A person, indeed, is a sacrifice. His first twentyófour years constitute the morning libation. The Gayatri metre has twentyófour syllables and the morning libation is offered with Gayatri hymns. The Vasus are connected with that part of the sacrifice. The pranas are the Vasus; for, verily, they make everything abide (visayanti) in this body.
verses.verse_skt: पुरुषो वाव यज्ञस्तस्य यानि चतुर्विशति वर्षाणि तत्प्रातःसवनं चतुर्विशत्यक्षरा गायत्री गायत्रं प्रातःसवनं तदस्य वसवोऽन्वायत्ताः प्राणा वाव वसव एते हीद सर्वं वासयन्ति॥
verses.verse_tl: puruṣo vāva yajñastasya yāni caturviśati varṣāṇi tatprātaḥsavanaṁ caturviśatyakṣarā gāyatrī gāyatraṁ prātaḥsavanaṁ tadasya vasavo'nvāyattāḥ prāṇā vāva vasava ete hīda sarvaṁ vāsayanti||

verses.meaning_en: If anything ails him during that period, he should recite the following mantra: "O ye pranas, ye Vasus, unite this morning libation with the midday libation. May I, who am a sacrifice, not disappear in the midst of the pranas, who are the Vasus." Thus he rises from his illness and becomes free of it.
verses.verse_skt: तं चेदेतस्मिन्वयसि किंचिदुपतपेत्स ब्रूयात्प्राणा वसव इदं मे प्रातःसवनं माध्यन्दिन सवनमनुसंतनुतेति माऽहं प्राणानां वसूनां मध्ये यज्ञो विलोप्सीयेत्युद्धैव तत एत्यगदो ह भवति॥
verses.verse_tl: taṁ cedetasminvayasi kiṁcidupatapetsa brūyātprāṇā vasava idaṁ me prātaḥsavanaṁ mādhyandina savanamanusaṁtanuteti mā'haṁ prāṇānāṁ vasūnāṁ madhye yajño vilopsīyetyuddhaiva tata etyagado ha bhavati ||

verses.meaning_en: His next fortyófour years constitute the midday libation. The Tristubh metre has fortyófour syllables and the midday libation is offered with Tristubh hymns. The Rudras are connected with that part of the sacrifice. The pranas are the Rudras; for, verily, they make everything weep (rodayanti).
verses.verse_skt: अथ यानि चतुश्चत्वारिशद्वर्षाणि तन्माध्यन्दिन सवनं चतुश्चत्वारिशदक्षरा त्रिष्टुप् त्रैष्टुभं माध्यन्दिन सवनं तदस्य रुद्रा अन्वायत्ताः प्राणा वाव रुद्रा एते हीद सर्व रोदयन्ति॥
verses.verse_tl: atha yāni catuścatvāriśadvarṣāṇi tanmādhyandina savanaṁ catuścatvāriśadakṣarā triṣṭup traiṣṭubhaṁ mādhyandina savanaṁ tadasya rudrā anvāyattāḥ prāṇā vāva rudrā ete hīda sarva rodayanti||

verses.meaning_en: If anything ails him during that second period, he should recite the following mantra: "O ye pranas, ye Rudras, unite this midday libation with the third libation. May I, who am a sacrifice, not disappear in the midst of the pranas, who are the Rudras." Thus he rises from his illness and becomes free of it.
verses.verse_skt: तं चेदेतस्मिन्वयसि किंचिदुपतपेत्स ब्रूयात्प्राणा रुद्रा इदं मे माध्यन्दिन सवनं तृतीयसवनमनुसंतनुतेति माऽहं प्राणाना रुद्राणां मध्ये यज्ञो विलोप्सीयेत्युद्धैव तत एत्यगदो ह भवति॥
verses.verse_tl: taṁ cedetasminvayasi kiṁcidupatapetsa brūyātprāṇā rudrā idaṁ me mādhyandina savanaṁ tṛtīyasavanamanusaṁtanuteti mā'haṁ prāṇānā rudrāṇāṁ madhye yajño vilopsīyetyuddhaiva tata etyagado ha bhavati ||

verses.meaning_en: His next fortyóeight years constitute the third oblation. The Jagati metre has fortyóeight syllables and the third oblation is offered with Jagati hymns. The Adityas are connected with that part of the sacrifice. The pranas are the Adityas; for, verily, they take up (adadate) everyóthing.
verses.verse_skt: अथ यान्यष्टाचत्वारिशद्वर्षाणि तत्तृतीयसवनमष्टाचत्वारिशदक्षरा जगती जागतं तृतीयसवनं तदस्यादित्या अन्वायत्ताः प्राणा वावाऽऽदित्या एते हीद सर्वमाददते॥
verses.verse_tl: atha yānyaṣṭācatvāriśadvarṣāṇi tattṛtīyasavanamaṣṭācatvāriśadakṣarā jagatī jāgataṁ tṛtīyasavanaṁ tadasyādityā anvāyattāḥ prāṇā vāvā''dityā ete hīda sarvamādadate||

verses.meaning_en: If anything ails him during that third period, he should recite the following mantra: "O ye pranas, ye Adityas, extend this my third libation to the full age. May I, who am a sacrifice, not disappear in the midst of the pranas, who are the Adityas." Thus he rises from his illness and becomes free of it.
verses.verse_skt: तं चेदेतस्मिन्वयसि किंचिदुपतपेत्स ब्रूयात्प्राणा आदित्या इदं मे तृतीयसवनमायुरनुसंतनुतेति माऽहं प्राणानामादित्यानां मध्ये यज्ञो विलोप्सीयेत्युद्धैव तत एत्यगदो हैव भवति॥
verses.verse_tl: taṁ cedetasminvayasi kiṁcidupatapetsa brūyātprāṇā ādityā idaṁ me tṛtīyasavanamāyuranusaṁtanuteti mā'haṁ prāṇānāmādityānāṁ madhye yajño vilopsīyetyuddhaiva tata etyagado haiva bhavati ||

verses.meaning_en: Mahidasa, the son of Itara, knew this and said addressing a disease: "O you disease! Why do you afflict me? I shall not die of this pain" He lived a hundred and sixteen years. He, too, who knows this lives on to a hundred and sixteen years.
verses.verse_skt: एतद्ध स्म वै तद्विद्वानाह महिदास ऐतरेयः स किं म एतदुपतपसि योऽहमनेन न प्रेष्यामीति स ह षोडशं वर्षशतमजीवत्प्र ह षोडशं वर्षशतं जीवति य एवं वेद॥
verses.verse_tl: etaddha sma vai tadvidvānāha mahidāsa aitareyaḥ sa kiṁ ma etadupatapasi yo'hamanena na preṣyāmīti sa ha ṣoḍaśaṁ varṣaśatamajīvatpra ha ṣoḍaśaṁ varṣaśataṁ jīvati ya evaṁ veda ||

verses.meaning_en: When a man hungers, thirsts and abstains from pleasures- these are his initiatory rites.
verses.verse_skt: स यदशिशिषति यत्पिपासति यन्न रमते ता अस्य दीक्षाः॥
verses.verse_tl: sa yadaśiśiṣati yatpipāsati yanna ramate tā asya dīkṣāḥ ||

verses.meaning_en: When he eats, drinks and enjoys pleasures, he then participates in Upasadas.
verses.verse_skt: अथ यदश्नाति यत्पिबति यद्रमते तदुपसदैरेति॥
verses.verse_tl: atha yadaśnāti yatpibati yadramate tadupasadaireti ||

verses.meaning_en: When a man laughs, eats and enjoys sexual intercourse-these are Stuta and Sastra.
verses.verse_skt: अथ यद्धसति यज्जक्षति यन्मैथुनं चरति स्तुतशस्त्रैरेव तदेति॥
verses.verse_tl: atha yaddhasati yajjakṣati yanmaithunaṁ carati stutaśastraireva tadeti ||

verses.meaning_en: Austerity, almsgiving, uprightness, non-violence and truthfulness-these are the gifts (daxina) for the priests.
verses.verse_skt: अथ यत्तपो दानमार्जवमहि सा सत्यवचनमिति ता अस्य दक्षिणाः॥
verses.verse_tl: atha yattapo dānamārjavamahi sā satyavacanamiti tā asya dakṣiṇāḥ||

verses.meaning_en: Because the life of a man is a sacrifice therefore they say that his mother will give birth (soshyati) to him, or his mother has given birth (asoshta) to him. The same words are used in the Soma-sacrifice and mean: "He will pour out the Soma- juice" and "He has poured out the Soma-juice." This is his birth. His death is the Avabhritha.
verses.verse_skt: तस्मादाहुः सोष्यत्यसोष्टेति पुनरुत्पादनमेवास्य तन्मरणमेवावभृथः॥
verses.verse_tl: tasmādāhuḥ soṣyatyasoṣṭeti punarutpādanamevāsya tanmaraṇamevāvabhṛthaḥ ||

verses.meaning_en: Ghora, of the line of Angirasa, communicated this teaching to Krishna, the son of Devaki-and it quenched Krishna's thirst for any other knowledge-and said: "When a man approaches death he should take refuge in these three thoughts: 'Thou art indestructible (axata),' 'Thou art unchanging (aprachyuta),' and 'Thou art the subtle prana.' "On this subject there are two Rik-verses:
verses.verse_skt: तद्धैतद्घोर् आङ्गिरसः कृष्णाय देवकीपुत्रायोक्त्वोवाचापिपास एव स बभूव सोऽन्तवेलायामेतत्त्रयं प्रतिपद्येताक्षितमस्यच्युतमसि प्राणसशितमसीति तत्रैते द्वे ऋचौ भवतः॥
verses.verse_tl: taddhaitadghor āṅgirasaḥ kṛṣṇāya devakīputrāyoktvovācāpipāsa eva sa babhūva so'ntavelāyāmetattrayaṁ pratipadyetākṣitamasyacyutamasi prāṇasaśitamasīti tatraite dve ṛcau bhavataḥ||

verses.meaning_en: "They (i.e. the knowers of Brahman) see everywhere the Supreme Light, which shines in Brahman, which is all- pervading like the light of day and which belongs to the primeval Seed. 'Perceiving the higher light in the sun-which is above the darkness of ignorance-as the higher light in the heart, perceiving the Supreme Light which is higher than all lights, we have reached the Highest Light, the Sun, the most luminous among the gods, yea, we have reached the Highest Light, the Sun, the most luminous among the gods."
verses.verse_skt: आदित्प्रत्नस्य रेतस उद्वयं तमसस्परि ज्योतिः पश्यन्त उत्तर स्वः पश्यन्त उत्तरं देवं देवत्रा सूर्यमगन्म ज्योतिरुत्तममिति ज्योतिरुत्तममिति॥
verses.verse_tl: āditpratnasya retasa udvayaṁ tamasaspari jyotiḥ paśyanta uttara svaḥ paśyanta uttaraṁ devaṁ devatrā sūryamaganma jyotiruttamamiti jyotiruttamamiti||

verses.meaning_en: One should meditate on the mind as Brahman-this is said with reference to the body. One should meditate on the akasa as Brahman-this is to said with reference to the gods. Thus both-the meditation with reference to the body and the meditation with reference to the gods-are being taught.
verses.verse_skt: मनो ब्रह्मेत्युपासीतेत्यध्यात्ममथाधिदैवतमाकाशो ब्रह्मेत्युभयमादिष्टं भवत्यध्यात्मं चाधिदैवतं च॥
verses.verse_tl: mano brahmetyupāsītetyadhyātmamathādhidaivatamākāśo brahmetyubhayamādiṣṭaṁ bhavatyadhyātmaṁ cādhidaivataṁ ca ||

verses.meaning_en: That Brahman has four feet (quarters): speech is one foot, the prana (the nose) is one foot, the eye is one foot, the ear is one foot-this is to said with reference to the body. Now with reference to the gods: Agni (fire) is one foot, Vayu (air) is one foot, Aditya (the sun) is one foot and the quarters (disah) are one foot. This is the twofold meditation with reference to the body and with reference to the gods.
verses.verse_skt: तदेतच्चतुष्पाद्ब्रह्म वाक्पादः प्राणः पादश्चक्षुः पादः श्रोत्रं पाद इत्यध्यात्ममथाधिदैवतमग्निः पादो वायुः पाद अदित्यः पादो दिशः पाद इत्युभयमेवादिष्टं भवत्यध्यात्मं चैवाधिदैवतं च॥
verses.verse_tl: tadetaccatuṣpādbrahma vākpādaḥ prāṇaḥ pādaścakṣuḥ pādaḥ śrotraṁ pāda ityadhyātmamathādhidaivatamagniḥ pādo vāyuḥ pāda adityaḥ pādo diśaḥ pāda ityubhayamevādiṣṭaṁ bhavatyadhyātmaṁ caivādhidaivataṁ ca ||

verses.meaning_en: Speech is, indeed, a fourth foot (quarter) of Brahman of which the mind is a symbol. It shines and warms with the light of fire. He who knows this shines and warms with fame, with renown and with the radiance of Brahman.
verses.verse_skt: वागेव ब्रह्मणश्चतुर्थः पादः सोऽग्निना ज्योतिषा भाति च तपति च भाति च तपति च कीर्त्या यशसा ब्रह्मवर्चसेन य एवं वेद॥
verses.verse_tl: vāgeva brahmaṇaścaturthaḥ pādaḥ so'gninā jyotiṣā bhāti ca tapati ca bhāti ca tapati ca kīrtyā yaśasā brahmavarcasena ya evaṁ veda ||

verses.meaning_en: Prana (the nose) is, indeed, a fourth foot of Brahman. It shines and warms with the light of the air. He who knows this shines and warms with fame, with renown and with the radiance of Brahman.
verses.verse_skt: प्राण एव ब्रह्मणश्चतुर्थः पादः स वायुना ज्योतिषा भाति च तपति च भाति च तपति च कीर्त्या यशसा ब्रह्मवर्चसेन य एवं वेद॥
verses.verse_tl: prāṇa eva brahmaṇaścaturthaḥ pādaḥ sa vāyunā jyotiṣā bhāti ca tapati ca bhāti ca tapati ca kīrtyā yaśasā brahmavarcasena ya evaṁ veda ||

verses.meaning_en: The eye, indeed, is a fourth foot of Brahman. It shines and warms with the light of the sun. He who knows this shines and warms with fame, with renown and with the radiance of Brahman.
verses.verse_skt: चक्षुरेव ब्रह्मणश्चतुर्थः पादः स आदित्येन ज्योतिषा भाति च तपति च भाति च तपति च कीर्त्या यशसा ब्रह्मवर्चसेन य एवं वेद॥
verses.verse_tl: cakṣureva brahmaṇaścaturthaḥ pādaḥ sa ādityena jyotiṣā bhāti ca tapati ca bhāti ca tapati ca kīrtyā yaśasā brahmavarcasena ya evaṁ veda ||

verses.meaning_en: The ear, indeed, is a fourth foot of Brahman. It shines and warms with the light of the quarters. With fame, with renown and with the radiance of Brahman he shines and warms who knows this, yea, who knows this.
verses.verse_skt: रोत्रमेव ब्रह्मणश्चतुर्थः पादः स दिग्भिर्ज्योतिषा भाति च तपति च भाति च तपति च कीर्त्या यशसा ब्रह्मवर्चसेन य एवं वेद य एवं वेद॥
verses.verse_tl: śrotrameva brahmaṇaścaturthaḥ pādaḥ sa digbhirjyotiṣā bhāti ca tapati ca bhāti ca tapati ca kīrtyā yaśasā brahmavarcasena ya evaṁ veda ya evaṁ veda ||

verses.meaning_en: The sun is Brahman: this is the teaching. An explanation thereof follows: In the beginning this universe was non-existent. It became existent. It grew. It turned into an egg. The egg lay for the period of a year. Then it broke open. Of the two halves of the egg-shell, one half was of silver, the other of gold.
verses.verse_skt: आदित्यो ब्रह्मेत्यादेशस्तस्योपव्याख्यानमसदेवेदमग्र आसीत्‌। तत्सदासीत्तत्समभवत्तदाण्डं निरवर्तत तत्संवत्सरस्य मात्रामशयत तन्निरभिद्यत ते आण्डकपाले रजतं च सुवर्णं चाभवताम्‌॥
verses.verse_tl: ādityo brahmetyādeśastasyopavyākhyānamasadevedamagra āsīt | tatsadāsīttatsamabhavattadāṇḍaṁ niravartata tatsaṁvatsarasya mātrāmaśayata tannirabhidyata te āṇḍakapāle rajataṁ ca suvarṇaṁ cābhavatām ||

verses.meaning_en: That which was of silver became the earth; that which was of gold, heaven. What was the thick membrane of the white became the mountains; the thin membrane of the yolk, the must and the clouds. The veins became the rivers; the fluid in the bladder, the ocean.
verses.verse_skt: तद्यद्रजत सेयं पृथिवी यत्सुवर्ण सा द्यौर्यज्जरायु ते पर्वता यदुल्ब समेघो नीहारो या धमनयस्ता नद्यो यद्वास्तेयमुदक स समुद्रः।
verses.verse_tl: tadyadrajata seyaṁ pṛthivī yatsuvarṇa sā dyauryajjarāyu te parvatā yadulba samegho nīhāro yā dhamanayastā nadyo yadvāsteyamudaka sa samudraḥ |

verses.meaning_en: And what was born of it was yonder Aditya, the sun. when it was born shouts of "Hurrah!" arose, together with all beings and all objects of desire. Therefore at its rise and its every return shouts of "Hurrah!" together with all beings and all objects of desire arise.
verses.verse_skt: अथ यत्तदजायत सोऽसावादित्यस्तं जायमानं घोषा उलूलवोऽनूदतिष्ठन्त्सर्वाणि च भूतानि च सर्वे च कामास्तस्मात्तस्योदयं प्रति प्रत्यायनं प्रति घोषा उलूलवोऽनूत्तिष्ठन्ति सर्वाणि च भूतानि सर्वे चैव कामाः॥
verses.verse_tl: atha yattadajāyata so'sāvādityastaṁ jāyamānaṁ ghoṣā ulūlavo'nūdatiṣṭhantsarvāṇi ca bhūtāni ca sarve ca kāmāstasmāttasyodayaṁ prati pratyāyanaṁ prati ghoṣā ulūlavo'nūttiṣṭhanti sarvāṇi ca bhūtāni sarve caiva kāmāḥ ||

verses.meaning_en: He who, knowing this, meditates on the sun as Brahman- pleasant sounds will quickly approach him and continue to delight him, yea, continue to delight him.
verses.verse_skt: स य एतमेवं विद्वानादित्यं ब्रह्मेत्युपास्तेऽभ्याशो ह यदेन साधवो घोषा आ च गच्छेयुरुप च निम्रेडेरन्निम्रेडेरन्‌॥
verses.verse_tl: sa ya etamevaṁ vidvānādityaṁ brahmetyupāste'bhyāśo ha yadena sādhavo ghoṣā ā ca gaccheyurupa ca nimreḍerannimreḍeran ||

verses.meaning_en: There once lived a king named Janasruti, who was a great- grandson of Janasruta. He bestowed his gifts with respect, gave away liberally and cooked much food for the hungry. He built rest-houses every-where with the thought that people everywhere would eat his food.
verses.verse_skt: ॐ जानश्रुतिर्ह पौत्रायणः श्रद्धादेयो बहुदायी बहुपाक्य आस स ह सर्वत आवसथान्मापयांचक्रे सर्वत एव मेऽत्स्यन्तीति॥
verses.verse_tl: om jānaśrutirha pautrāyaṇaḥ śraddhādeyo bahudāyī bahupākya āsa sa ha sarvata āvasathānmāpayāṁcakre sarvata eva me'tsyantīti ||

verses.meaning_en: One night some flamingos were flying along. One flamingo said to another: "Hey! Ho! Short-sighted, Short-sighted! The radiance of Janasruti, the great-grandson of Janasruta, has spread to the sky. Do not touch it, lest it should burn you."
verses.verse_skt: अथ ह हसा निशायामतिपेतुस्तद्धैव ह सो ह समभ्युवाद हो होऽयि भल्लाक्ष भल्लाक्ष जानश्रुतेः पौत्रायणस्य समं दिवा ज्योतिराततं तन्मा प्रसाङ्क्षी स्तत्त्वा मा प्रधाक्षीरिति॥
verses.verse_tl: atha ha hasā niśāyāmatipetustaddhaiva ha so ha samabhyuvāda ho ho'yi bhallākṣa bhallākṣa jānaśruteḥ pautrāyaṇasya samaṁ divā jyotirātataṁ tanmā prasāṅkṣī stattvā mā pradhākṣīriti||

verses.meaning_en: The other replied: "Say, who is this person about whom you have spoken as though he were like Raikva, the man with the cart?" "What sort of person is this Raikva, the man with the cart?"
verses.verse_skt: तमु ह परः प्रत्युवाच कम्वर एनमेतत्सन्त सयुग्वानमिव रैक्वमात्थेति यो नु कथ सयुग्वा रैक्व इति॥
verses.verse_tl: tamu ha paraḥ pratyuvāca kamvara enametatsanta sayugvānamiva raikvamāttheti yo nu katha sayugvā raikva iti ||

verses.meaning_en: The short-sighted flamingo replied: "As in a game of dice, when the krita is won, the lower ones also are won, so whatever merits people acquire all accrue to that Raikva. As Raikva I describe him, too, who knows what Raikva knows."
verses.verse_skt: यथा कृतायविजितायाधरेयाः संयन्त्येवमेन सर्वं तदभिसमैति यत्किंच प्रजाः साधु कुर्वन्ति यस्तद्वेद यत्स वेद स मयैतदुक्त इति॥
verses.verse_tl: yathā kṛtāyavijitāyādhareyāḥ saṁyantyevamena sarvaṁ tadabhisamaiti yatkiṁca prajāḥ sādhu kurvanti yastadveda yatsa veda sa mayaitadukta iti ||

verses.meaning_en: Janasruti the great-grandson of Janasruta overheard this conversation. Immediately after getting out of bed, he said to his attendant: "Friend, did you speak of me as though I were Raikva, the man with the cart?" "What sort of person is Raikva, the man with the cart?" "As in a game of dice, when the krita is won, the lower ones also are won, so whatever merits people acquire all accrue to that Raikva. As Raikva I describe him, too, who knows what Raikva knows."
verses.verse_skt: तदु ह जानश्रुतिः पौत्रायण उपशुश्राव स ह संजिहान एव क्षत्तारमुवाचाङ्गारे ह सयुग्वानमिव रैक्वमात्थेति यो नु कथ सयुग्वा रैक्व इति॥
verses.verse_tl: tadu ha jānaśrutiḥ pautrāyaṇa upaśuśrāva sa ha saṁjihāna eva kṣattāramuvācāṅgāre ha sayugvānamiva raikvamāttheti yo nu katha sayugvā raikva iti||

verses.meaning_en: Janasruti the great-grandson of Janasruta overheard this conversation. Immediately after getting out of bed, he said to his attendant: "Friend, did you speak of me as though I were Raikva, the man with the cart?" "What sort of person is Raikva, the man with the cart?" "As in a game of dice, when the krita is won, the lower ones also are won, so whatever merits people acquire all accrue to that Raikva. As Raikva I describe him, too, who knows what Raikva knows."
verses.verse_skt: यथा कृतायविजितायाधरेयाः संयन्त्येवमेन सर्वं तदभिसमैति यत्किंच प्रजाः साधु कुर्वन्ति यस्तद्वेद यत्स वेद स मयैतदुक्त इति॥
verses.verse_tl: yathā kṛtāyavijitāyādhareyāḥ saṁyantyevamena sarvaṁ tadabhisamaiti yatkiṁca prajāḥ sādhu kurvanti yastadveda yatsa veda sa mayaitadukta iti ||

verses.meaning_en: The attendant searched for him and returned without finding him. Then the king said to him: "Listen, where a knower of Brahman is to searched for, look for him there."
verses.verse_skt: स ह क्षत्ताऽन्विष्य नाविदमिति प्रत्येयाय त होवाच यत्रारे ब्राह्मणस्यान्वेषणा तदेनमर्च्छेति॥
verses.verse_tl: sa ha kṣattā'nviṣya nāvidamiti pratyeyāya ta hovāca yatrāre brāhmaṇasyānveṣaṇā tadenamarccheti ||

verses.meaning_en: After proper search the attendant came upon a person who, lying underneath his cart, was scratching an itch. Humbly he took his seat near him and said: "Revered Sir, are you Raikva, the man with the cart?" "Oh yes, I am he," he answered. Then the attendant returned, saying to himself: "I have found him out."
verses.verse_skt: सोऽधस्ताच्छकटस्य पामानं कषमाणमुपोपविवेश त हाभ्युवाद त्वं नु भगवः सयुग्वा रैक्व इत्यह ह्यरा३ इति ह प्रतिजज्ञे स ह क्षत्ताऽ विदमिति प्रत्येयाय॥
verses.verse_tl: so'dhastācchakaṭasya pāmānaṁ kaṣamāṇamupopaviveśa ta hābhyuvāda tvaṁ nu bhagavaḥ sayugvā raikva ityaha hyarā3 iti ha pratijajñe sa ha kṣattā' vidamiti pratyeyāya||

verses.meaning_en: Then Janasruti the great-grandson of Janasruta took with him six hundred cows, a necklace and a chariot with mules and went to Raikva and said: "Raikva, here are six hundred cows, a necklace and a chariot with mules. Pray, revered Sir, teach me the deity whom you worship."
verses.verse_skt: तदु ह जानश्रुतिः पौत्रायणः षट्शतानि गवां निष्कमश्वतरीरथं तदादाय प्रतिचक्रमे त हाभ्युवाद॥
verses.verse_tl: tadu ha jānaśrutiḥ pautrāyaṇaḥ ṣaṭśatāni gavāṁ niṣkamaśvatarīrathaṁ tadādāya praticakrame ta hābhyuvāda||

verses.meaning_en: Then Janasruti the great-grandson of Janasruta took with him six hundred cows, a necklace and a chariot with mules and went to Raikva and said: "Raikva, here are six hundred cows, a necklace and a chariot with mules. Pray, revered Sir, teach me the deity whom you worship."
verses.verse_skt: रैक्वेमानि षट्शतानि गवामयं निष्कोऽयमश्वतरीरथो नु म एतां भगवो देवता शाधि यां देवतामुपास्स इति॥
verses.verse_tl: raikvemāni ṣaṭśatāni gavāmayaṁ niṣko'yamaśvatarīratho nu ma etāṁ bhagavo devatā śādhi yāṁ devatāmupāssa iti||

verses.meaning_en: To him the other said: "Ah, may the necklace and the chariot remain with you, O Sudra, along with the cows." Thereupon Janasruti the great-grandson of Janasruta took with him a thousand cows, a chariot with mules, a necklace and his own daughter, too and went to Raikva.
verses.verse_skt: तमु ह परः प्रत्युवाचाह हारेत्वा शूद्र तवैव सह गोभिरस्त्विति तदु ह पुनरेव जानश्रुतिः पौत्रायणः सहस्रं गवां निष्कमश्वतरीरथं दुहितरं तदादाय प्रतिचक्रमे॥
verses.verse_tl: tamu ha paraḥ pratyuvācāha hāretvā śūdra tavaiva saha gobhirastviti tadu ha punareva jānaśrutiḥ pautrāyaṇaḥ sahasraṁ gavāṁ niṣkamaśvatarīrathaṁ duhitaraṁ tadādāya praticakrame ||

verses.meaning_en: Janasruti said to him: "Raikva, here are a thousand cows, a necklace, a chariot with mules, this wife and this village where you shall dwell. Revered Sir, teach me."
verses.verse_skt: त हाभ्युवाद रैक्वेद सहस्रं गवामयं निष्कोऽयमश्वतरीरथ इयं जायाऽयं ग्रामो यस्मिन्नास्सेऽन्वेव मा भगवः शाधीति॥
verses.verse_tl: ta hābhyuvāda raikveda sahasraṁ gavāmayaṁ niṣko'yamaśvatarīratha iyaṁ jāyā'yaṁ grāmo yasminnāsse'nveva mā bhagavaḥ śādhīti ||

verses.meaning_en: Then considering her (the princess) as the door for imparting knowledge, Raikva said: "O Sudra! You brought these cows and other presents; this is good. But you will make me speak now only through this means (i.e. the princess)." These are the villages named Raikvaparna, in the country of Mahavrishas, where Raikva lived. Now Raikva said to the king:
verses.verse_skt: तस्या ह मुखमुपोद्गृह्णन्नुवाचाजहारेमाः शूद्रानेनैव मुखेनालापयिष्यथा इति ते हैते रैक्वपर्णा नाम महावृषेषु यत्रास्मा उवास तस्मै होवाच॥
verses.verse_tl: tasyā ha mukhamupodgṛhṇannuvācājahāremāḥ śūdrānenaiva mukhenālāpayiṣyathā iti te haite raikvaparṇā nāma mahāvṛṣeṣu yatrāsmā uvāsa tasmai hovāca ||

verses.meaning_en: "Verily, Vayu (the air) is the swallower (samvarga). For when fire goes out it is indeed swallowed by the air. When the sun sets it is swallowed by the air. When the moon sets it is swallowed by the air.
verses.verse_skt: वायुर्वाव संवर्गो यदा वा अग्निरुद्वायति वायुमेवाप्येति यदा सूर्योऽस्तमेति वायुमेवाप्येति यदा चन्द्रोऽस्तमेति वायुमेवाप्येति॥
verses.verse_tl: vāyurvāva saṁvargo yadā vā agnirudvāyati vāyumevāpyeti yadā sūryo'stameti vāyumevāpyeti yadā candro'stameti vāyumevāpyeti ||

verses.meaning_en: "When water dries up it is swallowed by the air. For indeed the air absorbs them all. So much with reference to the gods.
verses.verse_skt: यदाप उच्छुष्यन्ति वायुमेवापियन्ति वायुर्ह्येवैतान्सर्वान्संवृङ्क्त इत्यधिदैवतम्‌॥
verses.verse_tl: yadāpa ucchuṣyanti vāyumevāpiyanti vāyurhyevaitānsarvānsaṁvṛṅkta ityadhidaivatam ||

verses.meaning_en: "Now with reference to the body: Verily, the prana is the swallower. When a man sleeps, speech goes into the prana, sight goes into the prana, hearing goes into the prana and the mind goes into the prana. For indeed the prana absorbs them all.
verses.verse_skt: अथाध्यात्मं प्राणो वाव संवर्गः स यदा स्वपिति प्राणमेव वागप्येति प्राणं चक्षुः प्राण श्रोत्रं प्राणं मनः प्राणो ह्येवैतान्सर्वान्संवृङ्क्त इति॥
verses.verse_tl: athādhyātmaṁ prāṇo vāva saṁvargaḥ sa yadā svapiti prāṇameva vāgapyeti prāṇaṁ cakṣuḥ prāṇa śrotraṁ prāṇaṁ manaḥ prāṇo hyevaitānsarvānsaṁvṛṅkta iti||

verses.meaning_en: "These are the two swallowers: the air among the gods, the prana among the senses."
verses.verse_skt: तौ वा एतौ द्वौ सम्वर्गौ वायुरेव देवेषु प्राणः प्राणेषु॥
verses.verse_tl: tau vā etau dvau samvargau vāyureva deveṣu prāṇaḥ prāṇeṣu ||

verses.meaning_en: Once Saunaka of the line of Kapi and Abhipratarin, the son of Kaxasena, were being waited upon at their meal, when a brahmacharin begged food of them. They did not give him anything.
verses.verse_skt: अथ ह शौनकं च कापेयमभिप्रतारिणं च काक्षसेनिं परिविष्यमाणौ ब्रह्मचारी बिभिक्षे तस्मा उ ह न ददतुः॥
verses.verse_tl: atha ha śaunakaṁ ca kāpeyamabhipratāriṇaṁ ca kākṣaseniṁ pariviṣyamāṇau brahmacārī bibhikṣe tasmā u ha na dadatuḥ ||

verses.meaning_en: He said: "One God, Prajapati, swallowed the four great ones. He is the Guardian of the world. O descendent of Kapi, O Abhipratarin, mortals do not see Him though he abides in manifold forms. Verily, this food has not been given to Him to whom it belongs."
verses.verse_skt: स होवाच महात्मनश्चतुरो देव एकः कः स जगार भुवनस्य गोपास्तं कापेय नाभिपश्यन्ति मर्त्या अभिप्रतारिन्बहुधा वसन्तं यस्मै वा एतदन्नं तस्मा एतन्न दत्तमिति॥
verses.verse_tl: sa hovāca mahātmanaścaturo deva ekaḥ kaḥ sa jagāra bhuvanasya gopāstaṁ kāpeya nābhipaśyanti martyā abhipratārinbahudhā vasantaṁ yasmai vā etadannaṁ tasmā etanna dattamiti ||

verses.meaning_en: Sanaka of the line of Kapi, pondering on those words, went to the brahmacharin and said: "He is the self of the gods, the creator of all beings, with unbroken teeth, the eater, the truly wise one. They speak of His magnificence as great, because without being eaten, He eats even what is not common food. O brahmacharin, we meditate upon this Brahman." Then he said to the attendants: "Give him food."
verses.verse_skt: तदु ह शौनकः कापेयः प्रतिमन्वानः प्रत्येयायाऽत्मा देवानां जनिता प्रजाना हिरण्यदष्ट्रो बभसोऽनसूरिर्महान्तमस्य महिमानमाहुरनद्यमानो यदनन्नमत्तीति वै वयं ब्रह्मचारिन्नेदमुपास्महे दत्तास्मै भिक्षामिति॥
verses.verse_tl: tadu ha śaunakaḥ kāpeyaḥ pratimanvānaḥ pratyeyāyā'tmā devānāṁ janitā prajānā hiraṇyadaṣṭro babhaso'nasūrirmahāntamasya mahimānamāhuranadyamāno yadanannamattīti vai vayaṁ brahmacārinnedamupāsmahe dattāsmai bhikṣāmiti||

verses.meaning_en: They gave food to him. Now these five (i.e. the eater vayu and fire, the sun, the moon and water, which are its food) and those five (i.e. the eater prana and the organs of speech, the eye, the ear and the mind, which are its food) make ten. These together constitute the krita (the highest throw in a game of dice). On account of this similarity of ten, these ten are the food in the ten quarters and further, they are Virat, the eater of food, by which all this becomes seen. All this he sees and the eater of food he becomes, who knows this, yea, who knows this.
verses.verse_skt: तस्मा उ ह ददुस्ते वा एते पञ्चान्ये पञ्चान्ये दश सन्तस्तत्कृतं तस्मात्सर्वासु दिक्ष्वन्नमेव दश कृत सैषा विराडन्नादी तयेद सर्वं दृष्ट सर्वमस्येदं दृष्टं भवत्यन्नादो भवति य एवं वेद य एवं वेद॥
